Japanese: ２月　９日　午前１１時１５分 地方裁判所　被告人第１控え室
English: February 9, 11:15 AM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 1

Japanese: あの‥‥御剣さま。 私、なんと言ってよいのか‥‥
English: Excuse me... Mr. Edgeworth? I'm not really sure what to say...

Japanese: あやめさん。 休憩時間は２０分しかない。
English: Iris. We only have 20 minutes.

Japanese: あなたに聞いておかなければ ならないことが、２つあります。
English: There are two things which I need to ask you before we reconvene.

Japanese: ‥‥なんでしょうか。
English: ...Alright. I'll help you any way I can.

Japanese: １つめ。あなたは、あの晩‥‥ 本当に、奥の院に行かなかったか？
English: First, about that night. You really didn't go to the Inner Temple, correct?

Japanese: 先ほどの証人は、修験堂で あなたに会い、会話をしたと言う。
English: The last witness claims to have met and talked with you in the Training Hall.

Japanese: あなたか、毘忌尼さん‥‥
English: Either you or Sister Bikini...

Japanese: そのどちらかが、 いつわりの証言をしているのだ。
English: ...is lying.

Japanese: 御剣さま。 きのうも申し上げました。
English: Mr. Edgeworth. It is just as I said yesterday.

Japanese: 私‥‥事件が起こるまで、 葉桜院の、自分の部屋にいました。
English: Until the incident occurred... I was in my own room, in Hazakura Temple.

Japanese: ‥‥よろしい。 それでは、もう１つ。
English: ...Very well. The second thing, then.

Japanese: あの晩‥‥葉桜院の スノーモービルが使用されている。
English: That night, the temple snowmobile was used in between the time Sister Bikini...

Japanese: 毘忌尼さんが葉桜院に戻ってから、 入浴後、事件を目撃するまで‥‥
English: ...returned to the Main Hall and when she bore witness to the murder.

Japanese: おそらく、１０時３０分ごろから １１時までのあいだのこと、だ。
English: Sometime between 10:30 and 11 PM that night.

Japanese: スノーモービルを使ったのは‥‥ あなた、なのだろうか。
English: Were you the one who used the snowmobile?

Japanese: スノーモービルのキーは、 １本しかございません。
English: There is only one key for the snowmobile.

Japanese: それを使えたのは‥‥ 私だけ、です。
English: The only person who could have used it... was me.

Japanese: やはり、あなたが‥‥！
English: So it was you...!

Japanese: しかし‥‥いったい、なぜ？
English: But... why?

Japanese: ‥‥おぼろ橋に、 なんの用があったのですか？
English: ...What made you go out to Dusky Bridge?

Japanese: 申しわけありません、 御剣さま‥‥
English: I'm sorry, Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: ‥‥あやめさん‥‥
English: Iris...

Japanese: 今は‥‥申し上げることは できませんの。
English: I can't... tell you about that... yet.

Japanese: 修験者さまが‥‥
English: Not until her safety is confirmed...
Her?

Japanese: 修験者さまのご無事が、 ハッキリするまでは‥‥
English: The safety of the acolyte...
(The "acolyte", huh...)

Japanese: （真宵くんのこと、か‥‥？）
English: (She's must be talking about Maya...)

Japanese: あやめさん。今でもまだ、 私の目を見て、言えるだろうか。
English: Iris. Look me in the eye and tell me the truth.

Japanese: 『天流斎　エリスを 殺害していない』‥‥と？
English: Did you kill Elise Deauxnim?

Japanese: 私には‥‥たとえ どなたであろうとも‥‥
English: No matter who or what may come...

Japanese: いのちを奪うことなど、 できません。
English: I could never take a life.

Japanese: （やはり‥‥ここでは 《さいころ錠》は現れない、か）
English: (As I thought... no Psycholock...)

Japanese: ‥‥よろしい。
English: ...Very well.

Japanese: 私は、真実を 知るのがシゴトなのです。
English: It is my job to get to the truth.

Japanese: 近いうちに‥‥あなたにも それがわかるでしょう‥‥。
English: You'll discover this for yourself soon enough.

Japanese: 同日　午前１１時３６分 地方裁判所　第７法廷
English: February 9, 11:36 AM District Court Courtroom No. 7

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、 審理を再開しましょう。
English: ...Court will now reconvene.

Japanese: 狩魔検事。証人のほうは‥‥？
English: Ms. von Karma. Where is the witness...?

Japanese: 休憩時間中、傍聴席で挙動不審な オトコが取り押さえられたわ。
English: During the break, a man was detained for suspicious behavior in the gallery.

Japanese: 挙動不審‥‥？
English: Suspicious behavior...?

Japanese: なんでも、一心不乱に スケッチしていたそうよ。
English: He was sketching something... Very intensely.

Japanese: ‥‥被告人のスケッチは 禁止されているハズですが‥‥
English: Dare I ask what the witness was sketching when he was detained?

Japanese: オニのようなカオでムチを ふるう、恐ろしい女の絵だったわ。
English: He drew a terrifying woman, armed with a demonic face and a vicious whip.

Japanese: それは、どう考えても あなたのコトではな
English: I can only presume that his intention was to capture you--

Japanese: ‥‥はうッ！
English: ...Aaaah!

Japanese: とにかく、そのオトコを 引きずり出してやるわ。
English: Anyway, it's time to drag this pathetic excuse for an artist before the court!

Japanese: 天流斎　マシス！ カクゴを決めて、入廷なさいッ！
English: Laurice Deauxnim! I hope you're ready... Get in here!

Japanese: （どうやら‥‥また、 荒れ模様になりそうだな‥‥）
English: (It would seem that whip is going to see plenty more use today...)

Japanese: いてェ！
English: Ouch!

Japanese: ‥‥まず、あの絵は 法廷侮辱罪にあたいするわ。
English: ...Your sketch is in contempt of this court!

Japanese: なな、ナニすんだよ！ オレはただ、ゲージュツ的な
English: H-Hey! I was just artistically rendering...

Japanese: いててェ！
English: Ou-Ouch!

Japanese: 召喚状を持った法廷係官を見て、 逃げようとしたそうね。
English: You tried to run away from the bailiff who was trying to hand you your subpoena, correct?

Japanese: で、でもオレ！　山で修行中の いただけねえ芸術家のタマゴだぜ！
English: L-Look! I'm nothing but a fledgling artist, training out in the mountains!

Japanese: たまたま、ミドリ色の絵の具が なくなって、街へおりて来たワケ！
English: I'm only down here in the city because I ran out of green paint.

Japanese: ま。専門的には“ビリジアン” っていうんだけどさァ。
English: Well, to use the technical term for the color, "viridian".
...Larry...

Japanese: ここは、絵の具屋ではないが‥‥？
English: This isn't an art store, now is it...?

Japanese: わかってるよ！ オレだって、中学は出てるんだ！
English: I know! I graduated junior high, OK!?

Japanese: ホラ。やっぱり、ゲージュツは 《畑仕事》がキホンだと思うワケ。
English: Look. Art is all about working in the fields, isn't it?

Japanese: は、はたけしごと‥‥ですか？
English: Working in the fields...?

Japanese: 《フィールド・ワーク》と 言いたいのだろう‥‥おそらくは。
English: I presume he wanted to say "field work"... I hope.

Japanese: そ。それそれ！ さっすが、トモダチだよなァ！
English: Th-That's it! Thanks, buddy!

Japanese: （リカイできてしまう自分が、 いくぶんカナしい‥‥）
English: (It's kind of sad that I was able to understand his mangled train wreck of a sentence...)

Japanese: フラッとここに立ち寄ってみたら、 イカすモデルを見つけたワケよ。
English: I just happened to stop in here and found a wonderful new model!

Japanese: だから、オレ！　このサイバンとは ゼンっゼン、関係ねえから。
English: So see!? I've got nothing to do with this trial! At all!

Japanese: “まっ赤な他人”っていうの？ かなり、深紅色なワケ。
English: I expect all of your faces to be red when you realize this mistake! Bright red!

Japanese: ま。専門的には “クリムゾン・レーキ”って‥‥
English: Or, to use the technical term, "crimson lake".

Japanese: ‥‥いててて！
English: ...Ou-Ou-Ouch!

Japanese: いてていてていてていて！ いたたッ！
English: Ou-Ou-Ou-Ouch! Ouch!<Judge> Ou-Ouch!

Japanese: つべこべ言わず、 オトコらしく証言なさいッ！
English: Stop your pathetic blabbing and testify like a man!

Japanese: その前に、ついでみたいに 私をぶたないように！
English: Refrain from whipping me, Ms. von Karma! Cross-whipping is as bad as cross-checking.

Japanese: 証人！　あなたのせいですぞ！ さっさと証言しなさいッ！
English: Witness! That was all your fault! Testify, now!

Japanese: うううう‥‥。トンでもねえ コトになっちまったなあ‥‥。
English: Waaaah... This is almost too much for me...!

Japanese: ～事件当夜、目撃したこと～
English: -- What I Saw --

Japanese: オレ、あの晩は星を見てたわけ。 あの、山奥のロッジで。
English: I was at that lodge out in the mountains, looking up at the stars that night.

Japanese: そりゃ、橋のあたりへは 何回か足を運んだけど‥‥
English: I walked to the bridge a number of times, but...

Japanese: す、スノーモービルなんて 見かけなかったぜ！
English: I didn't see a s-snowmobile!

Japanese: あの晩はオレ、橋のトコロじゃァ ダレにも会わなかったんだ！
English: I didn't meet anyone at the bridge that night!

Japanese: 待っていた子にはフラれちまって。 お師匠サマには死なれちまって。
English: The girl I was waiting for didn't show up... My teacher died on me...

Japanese: オレってば、ヒトリぽっちなのさ。 なあ、御剣よォォ‥‥
English: I'm all alone now. Aren't I, Edgeyyyyy...!?

Japanese: 証人！　証言中、弁護人に 語りかけないように！
English: Witness! Please refrain from talking directly to the lawyers during your testimony!

Japanese: ど‥‥どうせ、オレなんか。 ちっぽけでくだらねえオトコよ！
English: I-I'm just a nobody! Nothing but a small, worthless man, aren't I!?

Japanese: その証拠に、なぜかオレだけ 名前も職業も聞かれなかったし‥‥
English: And why wasn't I asked for my name and occupation... or anything else...!?

Japanese: 弁護人。このヒトはなぜ、 こんなにヒガみっぽいのですかな？
English: Mr. Edgeworth. This man seems to have quite a severe inferiority complex.

Japanese: ‥‥最近、このオトコのせいで たてつづけに事件が起こった。
English: ...He's recently been the cause of numerous incidents.

Japanese: どうやら、さすがに それで気がついたらしいのだ。
English: I think he's finally realized for himself...

Japanese: 自分という人間が、いかに はたメイワクな存在か‥‥
English: ...just how much of a nuisance he has been to other people!

Japanese: ああ、そうだよ！　すべての事件の ウラには、オレがいるんだ！
English: Yeah, that's right! I'm behind everything, every case!

Japanese: 気をつけろよな！ オレにさわると、不幸になるぜ！
English: Watch out, OK! Just touching me will make you eternally unhappy!

Japanese: ‥‥とにかく、しめやかに 尋問をおねがいしましょうか。
English: ...Well then, let us proceed with the cross-examination. With no touching, thank you.

Japanese: 詳しいハナシは、それからです。
English: We can delve into other details at a later time.

Japanese: ～事件当夜、目撃したこと～
English: -- What I Saw --

Japanese: オレ、あの晩は星を見てたわけ。 あの、山奥のロッジで。
English: I was at that lodge out in the mountains, looking up at the stars that night.

Japanese: そりゃ、橋のあたりへは 何回か足を運んだけど‥‥
English: I walked to the bridge a number of times, but...

Japanese: す、スノーモービルなんて 見かけなかったぜ！
English: I didn't see a s-snowmobile!

Japanese: あの晩はオレ、橋のトコロじゃァ ダレにも会わなかったんだ！
English: I didn't meet anyone at the bridge that night!

Japanese: （どうやら‥‥あくまでも 事件と無関係だと言い張る気か）
English: (It seems that he's going to claim to have nothing to do with this to the end.)

Japanese: （とにかく、このオトコふぜいに 時間をかけるのも愚かしい‥‥）
English: (I don't want this guy to cost us any more time...)

Japanese: （見えすいたムジュンを暴いて、 先を急ぐべきかもしれぬ‥‥）
English: (I need to slice through his obvious contradictions and keep things moving along...)

Japanese: いきなり、どうしましたか？ 弁護人。
English: What ever is the matter, Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: ‥‥あまりにムジュンに 満ちあふれているので‥‥
English: This one single statement is so full of contradictions...

Japanese: 一瞬、めまいとコンランを。
English: For a moment there, I thought I was going to collapse.

Japanese: ぬううう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: あなた自身はどうなの？　証人。
English: So, witness?

Japanese: 自分の証言‥‥どこがムジュン しているか、わかってるのかしら？
English: Any idea as to where these contradictions in your testimony lie?

Japanese: 返答しだいでは、コイツが ダマっていないわ。
English: Depending on your answer... I may let my whip have its way.

Japanese: ええと、そうなァ‥‥。
English: OK, give me a minute...

Japanese: たとえば、あの晩は 雪だったから星は見えねえとか、
English: Well, it was snowing that night so I couldn't possibly have seen the stars.

Japanese: あのボロ小屋を《ロッジ》って のはどうなのか、とか、
English: That run-down shack is hardly a "lodge", is it...?

Japanese: そもそもオレ、星を見に 行ったワケじゃねえ、とかかな？
English: And even if the stars could be seen, it isn't like I was there to look at them, right?

Japanese: やればできるじゃない。
English: See? You can do it if you try.

Japanese: えへへへへ‥‥
English: Heh heh heh...

Japanese: いててててェ！
English: Ou-Ou-Ou-Ouch!

Japanese: ‥‥モンダイは、ただ１つ。
English: ...There is only one issue here.

Japanese: あなたが、おぼろ橋で何を見たか？ ‥‥話してもらおうかしら？
English: What you saw at Dusky Bridge.

Japanese: “何回か”‥‥というと？
English: "A number of times"...? How many?

Japanese: ５回ぐらい‥‥かな。 ２０分に１回、行ったから。
English: Maybe... five times? I went once every twenty minutes.

Japanese: ということは‥‥
English: Which means...

Japanese: キサマはあの晩、２時間近くも 《極楽庵》にいたわけか‥‥
English: ...you spent almost two hours at Heavenly Hall that night?

Japanese: まあ。“早く来い”って念じながら 待つのが、ホントの“コイ”だし。
English: You bet! Real love is about waiting with your heart in your hands!
Love, you say...?

Japanese: このオトコは、被告人を山小屋に 呼び出そうとたくらんでいたのだ。
English: It was this man's intention to summon the defendant to the small shack.

Japanese: ‥‥このような、脅迫状で。
English: ...Using this blackmail letter.

Japanese: きょ、キョーハクジョー‥‥！
English: B-Blackmail...!?

Japanese: いやいや！　それはオレの、 暑苦しいアイの
English: No, no! That was simply a product of overflowing love...

Japanese: あぎィィッ！
English: A-Agh!

Japanese: フケツでヒレツでフラチでウワキな マヌケにフヌケてニヤけたオトコ。
English: You huffy, puffy, loosey- goosey excuse for a whimpering whining wuss of a witness!

Japanese: それで‥‥‥ 何を目撃したのですかな？
English: So... what did you see? I hope for your sake you saw a snowmobile...

Japanese: フケツでヒレツでフラチでウワキな マヌケにフヌケてニヤけた証人は。
English: You huffy, puffy, loosey- goosey excuse for a whimpering whining wuss of a witness, eh!

Japanese: ええと。‥‥そうだなあ‥‥
English: Um... well... You see...

Japanese: （フケツでヒレツでフラチ呼ばわり は、気にならないようだな‥‥）
English: (Being called those names doesn't seem to bother him at all...)

Japanese: ‥‥矢張！ 本当に見なかったのか？
English: ...Larry! You really didn't see it?

Japanese: な。な。な。なんでだよ！ なんだよ！　わかんねえよ！
English: H-H-Hey! No need to hit your desk! I can hear you!

Japanese: ‥‥オレ、見てねえよ！ す、す、スノーモービルなんて！
English: ...I didn't see it! I didn't see a s-s-snowmobile!

Japanese: 矢張。“すのーもーびる” と言ってみろ。
English: Larry. Say "snowmobile" for me, please?

Japanese: す、す、す、スノーモービル。
English: S-S-S-Snowmobile.

Japanese: ‥‥キサマのココロに やましいコトがないのならば‥‥
English: If you truly have nothing to hide...

Japanese: なぜ、すんなり言えないのだッ！
English: ...then why are you stammering like you just flew over a cuckoo's nest!?

Japanese: う、うるせえよ！　なんだよ！ なんでだよ！　わかんねえよ！
English: Sh-Shut up! W-W-What is this!? I don't know! Don't ask me!

Japanese: （とにかく‥‥身近な ムジュンから攻めるしかないな）
English: (It seems that I'll need to start from a more obvious contradiction.)

Japanese: （このオトコだけは‥‥ 私が、トドメを刺してやる！）
English: (I'm going to strike the blow that will finally get him to spill the beans!)

Japanese: “ダレにも”‥‥？
English: You didn't meet anyone?

Japanese: ホントだよォ。 オレ、カンケーねえんだよォ！
English: That's right. Because I've got nothing to do with this!

Japanese: ビリジアンの絵の具を 買いに来ただけなんだよォ！
English: And I'm just here to buy some viridian paint, OK!?

Japanese: クリムゾン・レーキな他人 なんだよォォ！
English: Come on, I expect to see those crimson lake faces! Now!

Japanese: ゆさぶるだけ、ムダのようですね。
English: It would appear that simply pressing him isn't going to be enough, Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: ‥‥そのようだ。
English: ...Indeed.

Japanese: 矢張　政志‥‥。
English: Larry Butz...

Japanese: キサマが、自分を捨てたくなった キモチは、わからないでもない。
English: I can understand why you might want to throw your old life away.

Japanese: キサマ自身がろくでなしな上、 ろくでもない事件を引き起こす‥‥
English: You're pretty pathetic, and you cause all sorts of trouble...

Japanese: ‥‥ゴメンよぉ‥‥
English: I'm sorry...!

Japanese: だが‥‥自分がいかに、 はたメイワクな存在なのか‥‥
English: But... Having realized just how much of a nuisance you have been...

Japanese: それに気づいただけでも、１歩 前進した‥‥そう言えなくもない。
English: ...that could be considered a step in the right direction.

Japanese: 御剣ィ。もしかして‥‥ なぐさめてくれてるのかい？
English: Edgey! Are you... trying to console me?

Japanese: とても、そうは聞こえません。
English: It certainly doesn't sound that way to me.

Japanese: しかし‥‥
English: However...

Japanese: キサマの引き起こした事件から 目をそらすことは、ユルさないッ！
English: I cannot forgive you for simply turning away from the incidents you create!

Japanese: うううう‥‥。カンゼンに、 オレのせいになってるよ‥‥
English: Waaah... You're totally pinning this on me...

Japanese: ‥‥さて。事件当夜の 話にうつろう。
English: ...Now then. Let us talk about the night of the murder.

Japanese: 事件を発見した毘忌尼さんは、 成歩堂　龍一に通報をたのんだ。
English: Sister Bikini, after seeing the murder take place, asked Phoenix Wright to report it.

Japanese: そして‥‥成歩堂は、橋のたもとの 公衆電話へ向かったわけだが‥‥
English: Thus, he headed for the public phone by the bridge...

Japanese: そのとき、彼はおぼろ橋で、 《ある人物》に会っている。
English: There, he happened across a certain nefarious individual!

Japanese: 矢張　政志、キサマだ！
English: You, Larry Butz!

Japanese: そうよなァ。 いわゆる、オレよなァ。
English: That's right. Me, in the flesh.

Japanese: ぬうううう‥‥。 よろしいかな、証人。
English: Hmm... Listen carefully, witness.

Japanese: いかに、あなたが名前を変えても、 まったくイミはない。
English: It doesn't matter if you change your name.

Japanese: あなた自身がろくでなしな上、 ろくでもない事件を引き起こす‥‥
English: So long as you remain pretty pathetic, you will continue to cause these incidents!

Japanese: そのジジツに変わりはないのです！
English: That reality will not change!

Japanese: で、でもよォ！ じゃア、オレ‥‥どうすれば？
English: B-But...! What do you want me to do, then!?

Japanese: 矢張。変えるべきなのは キミ自身、なのだ。
English: Larry. What you need to change is your inner self.

Japanese: “あの晩、何を見たのか”‥‥？
English: "What you saw that night"...

Japanese: 本当のことを証言しろ。 キミにできるのは、それだけだ。
English: Testify truthfully! That is all you can do for now.

Japanese: 御剣‥‥オレ。
English: Edgey... I...

Japanese: オレ、なんか‥‥ 目がさめたような気がするよ！
English: I think... I've finally woken up!

Japanese: ま。気のせいかもしれねえけど。
English: Well. I guess I could still be sleeping...

Japanese: とにかく、オレ！ やってみるよ！　証言してみるよ！
English: But anyway! I'll do it! I'll testify!

Japanese: ま。うまくいかねえかも しれねえけど。
English: Well. I'm not sure this'll go especially well...

Japanese: それでは、証人。 ‥‥もう一度、おねがいします。
English: I'll ask again, then, witness.

Japanese: 事件当夜‥‥ 《あなたが見たもの》とは！
English: What did you see on the night of the murder?

Japanese: ～事件当夜、目撃したこと・２～
English: -- What I Saw, Pt. 2 --

Japanese: 山小屋に行ったのが９時ごろだから ‥‥１０時半すぎ、だったなァ。
English: I went to the shack at around 9, so it would have been about 10:30 PM...

Japanese: フトンの上に寝ころんでいたら‥‥ 目の前がマッシロに光ったんだよ！
English: I was lying under my bedding when a white flash almost blinded me!

Japanese: そして、窓のそとで‥‥おぼろ橋が メラメラ燃えてるじゃねえか！
English: I looked out the window... and Dusky Bridge was on fire!

Japanese: カミナリが鳴ってたけど、すぐに 見に行ったワケよ。オレとしては。
English: There was still some thunder, but I went right away to check it out.

Japanese: そこで、成歩堂に会ったワケ。 ‥‥オレとしては。
English: That's when I ran into Nick.

Japanese: ぬううううう‥‥
English: Hmmmm...

Japanese: それはまた、ずいぶん ハデなものを見ましたな。
English: You certainly saw quite a lot, didn't you?

Japanese: それで‥‥燃えた後の橋は どうなったのかしら？
English: So... what happened to the bridge after it caught on fire?

Japanese: 《橋人生》をまっとうした、 っていうのかなあ‥‥
English: It was like me after a three day stint chasing a girl...

Japanese: “完全に燃えつきた！” って感じだったね、アレは。
English: It totally burnt out. Like, almost totally gone.

Japanese: ‥‥現に、成歩堂がその燃えカスを わたろうとして、落っこちたし。
English: ...I mean, trying to cross the burning remains was what caused Nick to fall.

Japanese: なな、なんですって‥‥！
English: W-What did you say!?

Japanese: まあ‥‥イノチに別状はない。 カゼをひいただけだ。
English: Oh, don't worry... nothing life-threatening. He just caught a cold.

Japanese: ‥‥あいかわらず、運がいいのか 悪いのか、わかりにくいオトコ‥‥
English: As always, hard to know if he should be called lucky or unlucky...

Japanese: それでは！　弁護人。 尋問をおねがいしましょう！
English: Now, Mr. Edgeworth! Please commence your cross-examination.

Japanese: ～事件当夜、目撃したこと・２～
English: -- What I Saw, Pt. 2 --

Japanese: 山小屋に行ったのが９時ごろだから ‥‥１０時半すぎ、だったなァ。
English: I went to the shack at around 9, so it would have been about 10:30 PM...

Japanese: フトンの上に寝ころんでいたら‥‥ 目の前がマッシロに光ったんだよ！
English: I was lying under my bedding when a white flash almost blinded me!

Japanese: そして、窓のそとで‥‥おぼろ橋が メラメラ燃えてるじゃねえか！
English: I looked out the window... and Dusky Bridge was on fire!

Japanese: カミナリが鳴ってたけど、すぐに 見に行ったワケよ。オレとしては。
English: There was still some thunder, but I went right away to check it out.

Japanese: そこで、成歩堂に会ったワケ。 ‥‥オレとしては。
English: That's when I ran into Nick.

Japanese: （‥‥橋が燃えた話は、 きのうも聞いた）
English: (...He told me about the burning bridge yesterday.)

Japanese: （だが‥‥どうも、計算の合わない 部分があるような気がする）
English: (But there's still something that doesn't quite fit...)

Japanese: （どうやら‥‥）
English: (It looks like...)

Japanese: （まだ、すべてを 語っていないようだな‥‥）
English: (...despite his change of heart, Larry still isn't telling us the whole truth.)

Japanese: 橋のところへついて、１分もしない うちに、成歩堂が来たんだよ。
English: I arrived at the bridge, and Nick showed up less than a minute later.

Japanese: あんなデカいものが燃えるなんて、 二度と見られない、って思ってさ。
English: I thought I'd never get another chance to see something so big burning!

Japanese: １時間半‥‥あんなサムい ところで、何をしていたのだ？
English: What did you do out there in the cold for an hour and a half?

Japanese: ううーん。あえて言うなら、 『ワクワクしていた』‥‥かなあ。
English: Well, if you really must know... I was busy being excited. I guess.

Japanese: ぬうううううう。 “ワクワク”‥‥
English: Hmmmmm... "Excited"? Dare I even ask...?

Japanese: 待ち合わせのヤクソクは １０時だったんだけどさあ。
English: I set the meeting time as 10:00 PM, right?

Japanese: ホレ。待ちきれなくて。早く 来ちゃうかもしれない、と思うと。
English: But I couldn't wait. And I thought she might come early, too.

Japanese: ‥‥まあ。ケッキョク、 来なかったワケだが。
English: ...Well, it appears she didn't come at all, in the end.

Japanese: ‥‥そもそも“待ち合わせ”ですら なかったワケだけど。
English: Because they never arranged to meet in the first place, did they?

Japanese: うるせえ！　オレの思い出に ケチをつけるな！
English: Shut up! Don't go picking my fond memories apart!

Japanese: とにかく、あのときは ちょっと、シンパイしたぜ。
English: Anyway, I was getting a little worried.

Japanese: あやめちゃん、道に 迷ったのかと思ってさあ。
English: I thought maybe Iris had lost her way.

Japanese: それでオレ、２０分に１回ぐらい、 橋まで見に行ってたんだよ。
English: So every twenty minutes or so, I went out to the bridge.

Japanese: でも‥‥アヤシイものは 特に見あたらなかったなあ‥‥
English: But I didn't see anything particularly suspicious...

Japanese: しかたねえからオレ、小屋で ワクワクしてたワケ。
English: I didn't have anything else to do, so I went back to the shack to wait for her.

Japanese: その“光”というのは、 モチロン‥‥
English: This "light" was, of course...

Japanese: カミナリよ。もお、 ピシャーン！　って。
English: Lightning. Like... KERPOW!

Japanese: ナオミのビンタ‥‥ぐらい スゴかったかもなあ、アレ。
English: Like a slap from Naomi, honestly! A big bada-boom!

Japanese: はがわわわわわわッ！
English: Hagaaaaah!

Japanese: ‥‥こんな感じね、きっと。
English: ...Or a little like that?

Japanese: うううう‥‥そこまで行ったら キョウコのパンチだよ‥‥
English: Waaah! That's more like a punch from Miranda!

Japanese: 証人は、橋に落雷した 瞬間は、見たのですかな？
English: Witness, did you actually see the lightning hit the bridge?

Japanese: いやー。オレ、その光にビックリ したから見たんだよ。‥‥窓を。
English: Well, I was a bit startled by the flash of light, so...

Japanese: それを見て‥‥キミは どうしたのか？
English: Seeing that, what did you do?

Japanese: そりゃア、オマエ！ オレも燃えたよ、メラメラとさァ！
English: What do you think!? I was burning up as well! From the fire in my heart!

Japanese: だから、橋を見に行った‥‥と？
English: And that's why you went to take a look at the bridge?

Japanese: ま、まあ。ジツを言うと‥‥
English: Well. To be honest...

Japanese: サムかったし、最初は “ほっとくか”って思ったけど‥‥
English: It was freezing cold, so at first I thought, "Forget it. I'm not leaving my covers."

Japanese: 雪も、もうほとんどやんでたから。 ‥‥その。気が変わったんだ。
English: But it had pretty much stopped snowing... So, I dunno, I changed my mind.

Japanese: ぬぬうううううう。
English: Hmmmmmm.

Japanese: 最初に“ほっとくか”という発想が 浮かぶのは、どうかと思いますな。
English: I'm not sure I care for the "forget it" attitude you had at first, witness.

Japanese: “すぐに”というのは ‥‥どれぐらいだ？
English: You said "right away"... But exactly how long after the strike was that?

Japanese: そうだなあ‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: カミナリが落ちて、橋に火がついて ‥‥５分ぐらいだったかなあ。
English: The lightning fell, and then the bridge caught on fire... Maybe around five minutes?

Japanese: まあ‥‥急に『見に行こう！』って 思ったワケね。コレが。
English: I mean. I suddenly thought, "Gotta go check this out!"

Japanese: その小屋から橋までは、 どれぐらいのキョリが‥‥？
English: How far is this small shack you were in from the bridge?

Japanese: そうなぁ。あのときは、 もう雪もやんでいたから‥‥
English: Hold on... Well, it had pretty much stopped snowing...

Japanese: ５分もあれば、ラクに 行けたと思うけど‥‥。
English: I guess about a five-minute walk?

Japanese: それで‥‥キミが目の前にした、 おぼろ橋の状況は？
English: And how did Dusky Bridge look when you got there?

Japanese: まあ、《宴たけなわ》って ヤツだったなー。
English: Like I had recovered a piece of my childhood.

Japanese: 小学生たちがキャンプファイヤー やりたがるのも、ムリねえわ。
English: I mean, not even the bonfires kids make during school camping trips can compare!

Japanese: （‥‥どうだろう。もう少し、 詳しく聞いてみるか‥‥？）
English: (...Well? Should I press him for a little more info?)

Japanese: （‥‥これ以上、イミのある ハナシは聞けそうもない‥‥）
English: (I don't think we can expect any more relevant information...)

Japanese: （ここは、他の情報を探ったほうが よさそうだな‥‥）
English: (Probably best to turn to other leads.)

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、証人。 証言をつづけてください。
English: ...Witness. Please continue your testimony.

Japanese: ‥‥矢張。１つだけ、 聞いておこう。
English: ...Larry. Let me ask you one thing.

Japanese: ナニよ？　御剣。 すずしいカオしちゃってさァ。
English: What is it, Edgey? What's with the serious face?

Japanese: キミは‥‥なぜ、 人を呼ばなかったのだ？
English: Why didn't you call anyone?

Japanese: え？　どういうことよ。
English: Eh? What do you mean?

Japanese: ふつう‥‥それだけの火災を 見たら、人に知らせるだろう。
English: Normally... when faced with a "towering inferno", one would try and tell someone.

Japanese: おぼろ橋のかたわらには、 公衆電話もあったのだからな。
English: There is a public phone right by Dusky Bridge, correct?

Japanese: ま。‥‥トーゼン、 オレもそうしようと思ったケド。
English: Well, of course I thought of doing that.

Japanese: それでは‥‥通報しなかった 理由を証言してもらいましょう！
English: So then... let's hear why you didn't!

Japanese: え‥‥ええ？　そうだなあ。 理由‥‥。りゆう‥‥
English: H-Huh? Yeah, OK. A reason... My reason...

Japanese: 《通報しなかった》というより、 するヒマがなかった‥‥のかなァ。
English: It isn't that I didn't try to tell anyone... I just didn't have time to, OK?

Japanese: “急に『見に行こう』と思った” ということは‥‥
English: So you suddenly thought to "check out" the bridge...?

Japanese: 最初は、“見に行くつもりは なかった”ということになる。
English: Does this mean that you initially had no intention of doing so?

Japanese: ま、まあ‥‥そういうコト、 なのかなあ‥‥
English: W-Well... Yeah, I guess it does...

Japanese: たしかに、サムかったし‥‥ ソトに出たくなかったなァ。
English: It was really cold... I didn't really want to go out there.

Japanese: それならば‥‥
English: If that's the case...

Japanese: なぜ、見に行くことにしたのか ‥‥聞かせてもらいたいな。
English: Why did you change your mind? I would like to hear your reasoning.

Japanese: それでは‥‥その理由を 証言してもらいましょう！
English: In which case... please give testimony to that effect, witness!

Japanese: え‥‥ええ？　そうだなあ。 理由‥‥。りゆう、かあ‥‥。
English: M-My reasoning... OK... Reasoning, is it...?

Japanese: そのときの成歩堂は‥‥どんな ようすだっただろうか？
English: How did Phoenix Wright look when you met him?

Japanese: ううーん。なんて言うか、 『ぼろゾーキン』みたいな感じ？
English: Hmm... He looked like a beaten up mule. He was dead tired.

Japanese: 葉桜院から、全速力で 走ってきたらしいから。
English: I mean, he had run all the way from Hazakura Temple.

Japanese: しばらくオレをニラみつけて ハアハア言ってたよ。
English: He simply stood, staring at me, breathing hard for a moment.

Japanese: 葉桜院までは‥‥どれぐらいの キョリがあるのかしら？
English: How far is it to Hazakura Temple from the bridge?

Japanese: アイツの足なら‥‥ 走って１５分ぐらい、かなあ。
English: On his legs... a 15 minute run, I'd say.

Japanese: ‥‥それで、殺人事件を 通報したワケ、ですか。
English: ...And that's when the murder was reported?

Japanese: そう！　で、ついでに 橋から転げ落ちていったワケ。
English: That's right! And then he took a fall from the bridge!

Japanese: 成歩堂と、ほとんど同時に 橋についた、と‥‥？
English: You claim to have arrived at the bridge at the same time as Wright?

Japanese: そ、そうなんだよ！
English: Y-Yeah!

Japanese: オレも、最初は《ダレかに 知らせなきゃ》って思ってたケド。
English: I thought, "I'd better tell someone about this."

Japanese: 成歩堂のヤツが《殺人事件だ！》 なんて騒ぎやがったから。
English: But then Nick came up yelling about murder!

Japanese: それでオレも、橋のコトは 忘れちまったワケ。
English: It totally made me forget about the bridge.

Japanese: もう、とっくに火も消えてたし。
English: The fire was pretty much out by then, anyway.

Japanese: （なんだ‥‥？　この、 ハゲしく不快な胸さわぎは‥‥）
English: (What's this feeling...? I suddenly have a terrible case of unease...!)

Japanese: そして‥‥警察に通報したあと、 成歩堂　龍一は落下したワケね？
English: It was after contacting the police that Phoenix Wright fell from the bridge, correct?

Japanese: ま。当たらずとも遠からず、 って感じだな、それで。
English: Yeah. That's pretty much it. More or less.

Japanese: ‥‥それで、 見に行くことにした、と？
English: ...So you decided to go and see what was happening?

Japanese: そうよ！　巨大な吊り橋が ガンガン燃えてるんだぜ！
English: That's right! We're talking about a massive suspension bridge burning to high heaven!

Japanese: これはもう、アレだろ？ なんだ、その。スペ‥‥すぺ‥‥‥
English: That's not something you see every day. A real spec... specule...

Japanese: スペシャル、だろ？　ある種。
English: Like, really special?

Japanese: （《スペクタクル》というコトバが 出てこなかったようだな‥‥）
English: (Can we really trust a witness who is unable to pronounce "spectacle"?)

Japanese: ‥‥なんというか‥‥ バチ当たりな青年ですな。
English: Life seems to love hitting this poor witness below the belt...

Japanese: ま。《人生、体当たり》が オレのテーマだから。
English: Well, my motto is to "hit life back as hard as I can."

Japanese: あぎぎィィッ！
English: A-Agah!

Japanese: ‥‥ついでに、ムチも 当てておいてあげるわ。
English: ...I'll give you a few hits too, if you'd like. With my whip.

Japanese: ‥‥存在自体がムジュンだらけの キサマに、理解できるかどうか‥‥
English: Your very existence being a contradiction, I'm not sure if you can grasp this or not...

Japanese: なんだよ！　ヒトを 宇宙人みたいに言うな！
English: What the hey, Edgey!? You make me sound like some sort of alien!

Japanese: キサマの証言は、決定的に ムジュンしている。
English: But your testimony is conclusively contradictory.

Japanese: 問題は‥‥《時間》だ。
English: The problem here... is time.

Japanese: あー、オレ。そういうの ニガテなんだよ。
English: I've never been the best timekeeper, you know.

Japanese: 『まさしくんが出かけた３分後、 自転車で追いかけました』
English: "Three minutes after Billy leaves on foot, you follow him on your bicycle."

Japanese: 『さて、追いつくのは、何分後 でしょう？』‥‥みたいなヤツ。
English: "How long does it take for you to catch up with him?" Terrible at those.

Japanese: ‥‥もっとカンタンな話だ。
English: ...This is much more simple.

Japanese: キミは、おぼろ橋にカミナリが 落ちるのを見て‥‥
English: You saw the lightning strike Dusky Bridge...

Japanese: すぐ、橋のところまで 見に行った。‥‥まちがいないな？
English: ...and immediately went to see what had happened. ...Is this correct?

Japanese: そうなァ。‥‥まあ、５分ぐらい グズグズしてたけど。
English: Yeah... Well, I wasted about five minutes first, but more or less.

Japanese: ‥‥ここに、事件当夜の 気象データがある。
English: ...I have the weather data from the night of the murder here.

Japanese: これによると、落雷の時刻は 午後１０時４５分。
English: According to this, the lightning fell at 10:45 PM.

Japanese: 小屋からおぼろ橋までのキョリは、 ５分弱。すると、キミは‥‥
English: You say it takes less than five minutes from the shack to Dusky Bridge.

Japanese: 午後１１時には、 橋のたもとにいたことになる。
English: Meaning you probably got there at around 11 PM.

Japanese: ‥‥ま。そんなもんだろうな、 たしかに。
English: ...That all sounds about right, I guess.

Japanese: そこへ、成歩堂がやって来て 落っこちたワケね。
English: And then Nick showed up and did his falling act.

Japanese: それは、あり得ないのだ。
English: That is impossible.

Japanese: ‥‥どういうことかしら？
English: ...What do you mean?

Japanese: １１時といえば、葉桜院で 事件が発生した時刻だ。
English: 11 PM is when the murder occurred in Hazakura Temple.

Japanese: つまり‥‥成歩堂はまだ、 葉桜院にいた。
English: Thus... Wright was still there, in the courtyard.

Japanese: 矢張　政志が、おぼろ橋で 彼に会えるはずがない！
English: There is no way that Larry could have encountered him at Dusky Bridge at that time!

Japanese: あ。はい！　あの。オレ。 異議あり、なんですけど。
English: Ah! Excuse me! I-I have an objection!

Japanese: なんですか？
English: You do?

Japanese: だから、御剣！　なんべんも 言わせるんじゃねえよ！
English: Edgey! How many times do I have to say this!?

Japanese: オレは、ヤハリじゃねえ！ 天流斎　マシス、そのひと
English: I'm not Larry! I'm Laurice Deauxnim!

Japanese: ぎゃはああッ！
English: Gyaaah!

Japanese: ‥‥事件が発見されたのが１１時 だと、立証はされていない。
English: ...It has not been proven that the murder occurred at 11 PM.

Japanese: ジューショクさまも『１１時ごろ』 としか、言っていないわ。 11".
English: The Sister only said, "around

Japanese: ‥‥それならば！　ジッサイは、 もっと早かったのかもしれない！
English: ...In which case! It could have been earlier than that!

Japanese: ‥‥アマいな、狩魔　冥‥‥
English: ...Watch your footing there, Ms. Franziska von Karma. The slope ahead is slippery!

Japanese: いずれにせよ、１１時に成歩堂が おぼろ橋にいたハズはないのだ。
English: For there is still no way that Wright could have been at Dusky Bridge at 11 PM!

Japanese: な‥‥なぜ！
English: And why not!?

Japanese: 気象データに、ハッキリと こう書かれている。
English: It is clearly written here in the weather data report.

Japanese: 橋が燃えつきるのにかかった 時間は‥‥《約３０分》。
English: It took around 30 minutes for the bridge to burn out.

Japanese: つまり！　橋は少なくとも １１時１５分までは燃えていた！
English: Therefore! The bridge must have been burning until at least 11:15 PM!

Japanese: それが‥‥どういうことに なるのですかな？
English: Which means... what, exactly?

Japanese: 成歩堂は、あの晩‥‥ 橋が燃えているのを見ていない。
English: Wright did not see the bridge as it was burning that night.

Japanese: 彼がついたとき‥‥おぼろ橋は、 燃えつきた後だった！
English: He did not arrive there until after the flames had died down!

Japanese: ‥‥矢張！
English: ...Larry!

Japanese: キサマが橋のたもとに 行ったのが、１１時。
English: You arrived at the bridge at 11 PM.

Japanese: 成歩堂が来たのが、 １１時１５分すぎ‥‥
English: Wright did not make it there until at least 11:15...

Japanese: キサマは‥‥またしても、 我々にかくそうとしている！
English: Are you still trying to hide something from us!?

Japanese: その、《空白の１５分》をッ！
English: What happened during these missing 15 minutes!?
Urk...

Japanese: オレ‥‥目がさめたような 気がしてたんだけど‥‥
English: I... I feel like I just woke up...

Japanese: やっぱり気のせいだったんだ よおおおおおおおおおおおん！
English: I guess I was still sleeping after all! Ha ha ha! Pinch me!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！ せいしゅくにィィィッ！
English: Order! Order! Order! Ordaaaaaaaaagh!

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂　龍一に会うまで、 １５分の空白があった‥‥
English: ...So there was a missing 15 minutes prior to meeting Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: そんなの、たいした 問題ではない！
English: I hardly see that as much of a problem!

Japanese: そうだよ！　たいした 問題じゃねえよ！
English: Yeah! Not much of a problem at all!

Japanese: ‥‥そうだろうか‥‥？
English: Really...?

Japanese: 目の前で、橋が燃えている。 すぐ横には、公衆電話もある。
English: The bridge is burning before your eyes, and there is a phone right next to it.

Japanese: それなのに‥‥通報もせず、 キサマは何をしていた？
English: Why, then, did you not report the accident?

Japanese: 橋が燃えているのを ながめていた‥‥のですかな？
English: Did you simply... watch the bridge burn?

Japanese: 問題は、そこだ‥‥
English: That is the problem here...

Japanese: このオトコは、燃えつきた あとも、橋のたもとにいた。
English: Even after the bridge burnt out, he was still there!

Japanese: そして、あいかわらず‥‥ 通報もせず、ボンヤリしていた。
English: He simply stood there and didn't report anything.

Japanese: そ‥‥そうなるわね‥‥
English: Th-That's what it sounds like...

Japanese: ‥‥いくらコイツが矢張といえど、 さすがにそれは、あり得ない‥‥
English: This might be Larry we are talking about, but even he is incapable of being so stupid.

Japanese: そこには、何か “理由”があったはずだ！
English: There has to be a reason for his inaction!

Japanese: ‥‥御剣ィ‥‥
English: Edgey...

Japanese: オマエ‥‥ついに、オレを ホンキにさせちまったな‥‥
English: I think it's about time I got serious with you, dude.

Japanese: ‥‥やはり、今まで ホンキではなかったようだな‥‥
English: ...Just as I thought, you've been playing with us all this time.

Japanese: いいのか‥‥？ オレ‥‥
English: Listen... I'm...

Japanese: オレ、しゃべっちまうぞ！ ホントに、いいんだな‥‥？
English: I'm going to tell you everything! Are you sure you want to hear it all!?

Japanese: う、うム‥‥‥
English: Y-Yes...

Japanese: ホントォォにオレ、今度こそオレ、 しゃべっちまうかもしれ
English: I may reeeeally say it this time! Everything!

Japanese: かはァァッ！
English: Kahaah!

Japanese: ‥‥さっさとしゃべる。
English: ...Then say it!

Japanese: それでは‥‥いささか よくない予感がしますが‥‥
English: Very well... I have a terribly bad feeling aboot this; however...

Japanese: 今度こそ、しめやかなる真実を 証言していただきましょう。
English: ...let's have the witness finally give us the whole truth.

Japanese: ‥‥《空白の１５分間》‥‥ あなたが、何をしていたか！
English: Now... for this 15 minute gap, what where you doing, witness!?

Japanese: ～空白の１５分間～
English: -- The Missing 15 Minutes --

Japanese: オレ、天流斎よ。ゲージュツカよ。 やるコトなんて、決まってるだろ！
English: I'm a Deauxnim. I'm an artist! What do you think I was doing?

Japanese: スケッチしてたんだ！　橋の前で！ もう、ムチュウで描きまくったぜ！
English: Sketching! In front of the bridge! I was whipped up into a frenzy of art!

Japanese: オレの感じたショーゲキを‥‥ 見たまま、ノートにぶつけたんだ！
English: The shock and awe that I was feeling... I transferred it all directly onto the page!

Japanese: ‥‥気がついたら火は消えていて、 ヤツがヨロめきながら現れたのさ。
English: ...Before I realized it, the flames had gone out and then he came running up.

Japanese: ぬぬぬうううううう‥‥
English: Hmmmmm...

Japanese: まあ‥‥芸術家なら しかたないかもしれません。
English: I suppose artists can be strange folk...

Japanese: そうだよ！　ゲージュツのためなら すべてをギセイにしていいんだぜ！
English: That's right! I'm willing to sacrifice everything in order to draw the perfect sketch!

Japanese: （‥‥自分で言うな！）
English: (...Including the truth, from the sound of it!)

Japanese: いかがですか？　弁護人。 疑問は、なくなりましたが‥‥？
English: Mr. Edgeworth. Has this removed the last of your doubts?

Japanese: ‥‥それはちがう、裁判長。 まだ、大きな疑問が残っている。
English: Not at all, Your Honor. One very large doubt still remains.

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥？
English: And what would that be?

Japanese: あくまでもコイツにしては、 わりとマトモな理由なのに‥‥、
English: This is a surprisingly believable story, especially considering the source...

Japanese: ‥‥なぜ、それを今まで かくしていたか？
English: So why did he think he needed to hide it from us until now?

Japanese: 弁護側は‥‥かならず、 それを暴いて見せる。
English: I intend to drag the reason out of him!

Japanese: クックックッ！　後悔するなよ、 御剣ィィィィ‥‥
English: Hah ha ha! You'll regret this, Edgey!

Japanese: ～空白の１５分間～
English: -- The Missing 15 Minutes --

Japanese: オレ、天流斎よ。ゲージュツカよ。 やるコトなんて、決まってるだろ！
English: I'm a Deauxnim. I'm an artist! What do you think I was doing?

Japanese: スケッチしてたんだ！　橋の前で！ もう、ムチュウで描きまくったぜ！
English: Sketching! In front of the bridge! I was whipped up into a frenzy of art!

Japanese: オレの感じたショーゲキを‥‥ 見たまま、ノートにぶつけたんだ！
English: The shock and awe that I was feeling... I transferred it all directly onto the page!

Japanese: ‥‥気がついたら火は消えていて、 ヤツがヨロめきながら現れたのさ。
English: ...Before I realized it, the flames had gone out and then he came running up.

Japanese: （‥‥どうやら、この尋問には、 大きな手がかりがあるようだ）
English: (There is a big clue waiting for me in this cross- examination...)

Japanese: （どう考えても、ふざけている としか思えない証言だが‥‥）
English: (This testimony does nothing less than mock the court...)

Japanese: （なんらかの真実が、ここに かくされているはずだ！）
English: (But I am sure that there is a vital kernel of truth hidden in it somewhere!)

Japanese: あやめちゃんが、飛んだんだよ！ 白いずきんをハタめかせて！
English: I saw Iris flying! Her white hood fluttering!

Japanese: それで、矢張‥‥
English: Tell us, Larry...

Japanese: だから、天流斎だってばよ！ オマエもしつこいなあ！
English: My name is Laurice! Get it right!

Japanese: そんなコトだから、オレみたいな モテモテになれねえんだよ！
English: Mistakes like that are what keep you from being popular with the ladies like I am!

Japanese: ケッキョク、 あなたは何者なのですか？
English: Just who exactly are you!?

Japanese: オレ、天流斎　マシス！ よろしくな！
English: I'm Laurice Deauxnim! Apprentice extraordinaire!

Japanese: 《自称》だが。
English: That's what he calls himself, in any case.

Japanese: ‥‥せめて、 芸術家なのでしょうな‥‥？
English: ...Then you are... an artist?

Japanese: アタリマエだろ！ アーチストよ、オレ！
English: Of course! I'm an artist, the real thing!

Japanese: 《自称》だが。
English: Yet again, that's what he calls himself.

Japanese: ‥‥名前に、イミなどない。
English: ...Names mean nothing.

Japanese: 問題は、ただ１つ。 ‥‥あなたが何をしたか、よ。
English: There is only one issue I care to discuss. What were you doing?

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥それが 大きな問題になるだろうな）
English: (That is a very big issue, indeed.)

Japanese: す、スケッチ‥‥！ 燃えている橋をか？
English: S-Sketching!? The burning bridge?

Japanese: 燃えてる橋や、 その他モロモロだよ！
English: The burning bridge and everything that came with it!

Japanese: なんだ？ “モロモロ”‥‥とは。
English: What? "Came with it"...?

Japanese: そ、それをオレの口から 言わせようってのかよ、御剣！
English: You want to hear this from my lips, do you, Edgey!?

Japanese: きっと後悔するぜ！　なにしろ オレのスケッチは、そんじょそこ
English: Y-You'll regret this! That sketch of mine is...

Japanese: ががああッ！
English: Gaaah!

Japanese: ‥‥もう、いいわ。
English: ...Enough.

Japanese: ‥‥見せなさい、証人。 その、バカげた“スケッチ”を！
English: Just take that ridiculous sketch of yours out already, witness!

Japanese: な。な。な。なんでだよ！ なんだよ！　わかんねえよ！
English: W-W-W-What are you talking about? I don't know what you mean!

Japanese: どうも、それがイチバン、 手っ取り早いようですな。
English: That does indeed appear to be the fastest solution.

Japanese: ‥‥いかがですか？　弁護人。
English: ...I'll leave it to you, Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: （どうするべきか‥‥？）
English: (What should I do...?)

Japanese: （その絵を見た瞬間‥‥世界が 終わってしまうような‥‥）
English: (I've got a terrible feeling that the instant this sketch is revealed...)

Japanese: （それほどまでに、 イヤな予感がするのだが‥‥）
English: (...the entire world may be changed by what is depicted there...!)

Japanese: ‥‥やめておくことを おすすめする。
English: ...I cannot recommend looking at the sketch.

Japanese: ソイツの《作品》には、 超高校級の破壊力があるからな。
English: His "works of art" are a force of nature best measured on the Richter scale.

Japanese: アタリマエだろ！ オレだって、中学は出てるんだ！
English: That's right! They'll shake your heart to pieces!

Japanese: ぬぬううううう‥‥
English: Hmmmm...

Japanese: たしかに、ストライクゾーンを 大きくハズしたものが予想される。
English: This courtroom has yet to meet earthquake standards! I think we'd better pass!

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。 他の手がかりを追ってみましょう。
English: ...In which case, let us turn to other leads.

Japanese: ‥‥矢張。そのスケッチ‥‥ 見せてくれないか。
English: ...Larry. I wonder if you could show us your sketch. Please?

Japanese: フッ。‥‥さすがのオレも、 予想できなかったぜ。
English: Well, well... Even I couldn't have imagined it'd turn out like this.

Japanese: ‥‥何がだ？
English: ...Imagined what?

Japanese: 天流斎　マシスのデビューが、 こんなカタチで実現するなんて‥‥
English: That Laurice Deauxnim's debut would take place here, today, like this!

Japanese: いたたはァッ！
English: Ou-Ou-Ou-Ou-Ouch!

Japanese: さっさと提出なさい！
English: Show it! Now!

Japanese: いいか‥‥見ておどろくなよ‥‥
English: OK... But steel yourselves!

Japanese: コイツが‥‥ 天流斎　マシスくんの世界だッ！
English: This... is the world of Laurice Deauxnim!
Ah.

Japanese: え、ええと‥‥その。
English: Um... Well...

Japanese: これが‥‥その。いわゆる 《おぼろ橋》、ですか。
English: So this... this is Dusky Bridge, correct?

Japanese: ず、ずいぶん大きな吊り橋ですね。 いかがですか、狩魔検事！
English: Q-Quite a large bridge, isn't it? Your response, Ms. von Karma?

Japanese: そそ、そうね‥‥
English: Y-Yes, well...

Japanese: お‥‥思ったより、 よく描けているわ。
English: I-It's a better drawing than I expected.

Japanese: だろ？　だろ？　キレイな火の色を 出すのに、苦労したんだぜ！
English: Isn't it? Isn't it? I struggled to reproduce those flames. I really did!

Japanese: ‥‥はあ、そうなんでしょうねえ。
English: ...Yes, I'm sure you did.

Japanese: （うううう‥‥気まずい‥‥）
English: (Nngh... This is going to get ugly...)

Japanese: （どうやら‥‥だれも、 切り出す勇気がないようだな）
English: (No one has the bravery to bring it up, it seems...)

Japanese: （‥‥この、 ナゾの飛行物体について）
English: (This... mysterious flying object...)

Japanese: ‥‥おい、矢張。
English: ...Larry.

Japanese: ナニよ。
English: What?

Japanese: 橋が燃えているのは、まあ いいとして、だ。
English: The burning bridge is fine.

Japanese: なんなのだ？　その上を ただよう、不吉なシロモノは‥‥
English: But... What is that unfortunate looking figure...?

Japanese: お。気づいちゃった？　やっぱり。
English: Ah. You spotted that? I thought you might.

Japanese: まあ‥‥目をそらすわけにも いかないからな。
English: However much I might want to ignore it... I can't.

Japanese: あやめちゃんだよ、あやめちゃん。 決まってるだろ！
English: It's Iris, of course! Iris!

Japanese: まったく、女の子なんだからさァ。 ムリしちゃダメだよなあ。
English: I wish she'd take better care of herself. We have to plan for our future, you know!

Japanese: ケガしたらどうするんだよ。 ‥‥空なんか飛んじゃってさ！
English: What would've happened to her if she had injured herself flying like that...?

Japanese: 矢張。これだけは正直に、 本当のコトを言ってくれ。
English: Larry, please. Answer this next question honestly.

Japanese: いいぜ。
English: OK.

Japanese: キサマ‥‥本当に、 見たと言うつもりか？
English: Are you REALLY claiming to have seen this?

Japanese: 炎上するおぼろ橋の 上空を飛びこえる‥‥
English: Are you claiming to have seen the silhouette of the defendant...

Japanese: ‥‥被告人の姿を。
English: ...flying over a bridge that was engulfed in flames...?

Japanese: ああ。見たぜ。
English: Yep. That's what I saw.

Japanese: だから、描いたんじゃねえか。 ゲージュツカだから。オレ。
English: That's why I drew it! I'm an artist! A real artist!

Japanese: そんな‥‥！
English: Are...!

Japanese: そんな‥‥！
English: You...!

Japanese: ‥‥そんな、バカなことがああッ！
English: High! The girl... She's really high up in this picture!

Japanese: うはははッ！
English: Uwaaah!

Japanese: なな、ナニをするんですか！
English: What was that for!?

Japanese: ‥‥これは、悪いユメに ちがいない‥‥
English: ...This is all a bad dream.

Japanese: それをたしかめるため、 ほっぺをムチでたたいてみたわ。
English: I was hitting you on the cheek to test that theory!

Japanese: たたくなら、 自分のほっぺにするように！
English: Please whip your own cheek from now on if you wish to test your wild theories!

Japanese: と‥‥とにかく！
English: A-Anyway!

Japanese: 法廷は、ゼッタイに 認めることはできません！
English: No court of law will ever acknowledge...

Japanese: ひひ、ひ、ヒトがそんな‥‥ 空を飛ぶ、なんて！
English: ...that p-p-people can f-f-fly!

Japanese: ‥‥かつて、１件だけ。 そんな事件をあつかったわ。
English: ...Actually, there is some precedent for this.

Japanese: ずいぶん高く飛んでたぜー、 あやめちゃん。
English: She was flying pretty high, my sweet Iris.

Japanese: 橋から１０メートルぐらい。 ありゃア、事件だったね！
English: She was about 30 feet above the bridge, at least! It was really something to see!

Japanese: きき、きっと、これは何かの まちがいです！
English: Th-This has to be some kind of m-m-mistake!

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人が、しめやかに さばいてくれるでしょう！
English: ...Mr. Edgeworth, please bring the witness back down to earth!

Japanese: え！　私が、だろうか‥‥？ Me...!?
English: What?

Japanese: この証人は、あなたの トモダチなんでしょう？
English: This witness is your friend, is he not?

Japanese: “連帯セキニン”ね。 ‥‥御剣　怜侍。
English: "Accessory to foolishness..." ...Miles Edgeworth.

Japanese: それでは、やや強引に 尋問に戻りたいと思います！
English: Let us get back to the cross-examination! By force, if necessary!

Japanese: 弁護人は、なんとしても ムジュンを暴くように。
English: Mr. Edgeworth, I expect you to expose the obvious contradiction here!

Japanese: う‥‥うム‥‥
English: Y-Yes, Your Honor...

Japanese: （‥‥また１つ、コイツの 思い出がふえたな‥‥）
English: (Looks like I've got another reason to remember this moron...)

Japanese: ‥‥あの。オレのデビュー作、 どうしたモンかなあ。
English: ...Well, what did you think of my debut piece?

Japanese: こっちに向けないようにッ！
English: Get that thing away from me!

Japanese: 《矢張のスケッチ》のデータを しぶしぶ法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Larry's Sketch added to the Court Record.

Japanese: では、尋問に戻りましょう！
English: Now, hurry up and cross-examine him!

Japanese: 燃える橋に、そんなに 感動したというのか？
English: The burning bridge moved you that much, did it?

Japanese: ‥‥どうも、考えにくいのだが。
English: ...I find that hard to believe.

Japanese: バカ野郎！　御剣、オマエ‥‥ オレのコト、わかってねえよ！
English: You moron, Edgey! You don't understand me at all!

Japanese: 捨てられた子ネコを見かけたら、 ナミダを流して通りすぎる‥‥
English: If I see an abandoned cat, I find myself passing by it with tears in my eyes!

Japanese: そんな、ムセキニンなやさしさに 満ちたオトコなんだよ、オレは！
English: I am a man overflowing with irresponsible kindness!

Japanese: そのやさしさを、ありったけ ノートにぶちまけたんだ！
English: And I use my irresponsible kindness to fill the pages of my notebook!

Japanese: “ノート”というのが 泣かせるわね‥‥
English: An artist, using a notebook... It's so pathetic, it makes me want to cry myself.

Japanese: スケッチブックぐらい 買いなさい！
English: I command you to go buy at least a proper sketch pad!

Japanese: なな、なんで そんなコトでぶたれるんだよ‥‥
English: W-W-What has that got to do with anything!?

Japanese: とにかく証人は、お絵かきに ムチュウだったわけですね？
English: I'm not sure if I care for an artist using a notebook for sketching. Proceed, witness.

Japanese: それまで‥‥時間を忘れて 描いていた、と‥‥？
English: You forgot the passage of time to that extent, did you?

Japanese: まあな。オレ、ムチュウになると、 ワケがわからなくなるから。
English: Well... Once I get caught up in something, I tend to forget everything else...

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥このオトコは、 ムカシからそうだった）
English: (That's true... He's always been that way.)

Japanese: （おそらく、この発言に ウソはないだろう‥‥）
English: (I guess he's not lying about that, at least.)

Japanese: 成歩堂　龍一が現場に 現れたのは、１１時１５分すぎ‥‥
English: Phoenix Wright appeared on the scene after 11:15...

Japanese: 絵を描いていたのなら、 なんの疑問もないわね。
English: If he was drawing until then, that dispels all doubts.

Japanese: ニンゲンとして、大きな ギモンを感じますが‥‥
English: Aside from doubts of his humanity, eh...

Japanese: キサマ‥‥それを どこから見たというのだ！
English: Just where did you see this sight from!?

Japanese: なんでドナるんだよぅ‥‥
English: Hey! No need to shout...

Japanese: ボロ小屋で、フトンの上に 寝っころがっていたときだけど。
English: When I was lying in bed in that run-down shack...

Japanese: オレ、カミナリの音で 橋が燃えたのに気づいて‥‥
English: The crack of the lightning bolt made me realize that the bridge was burning.

Japanese: しばらくは、ぼんやり見てたわけ。 『よく燃えるなあ』って。
English: I just watched it for a while from there, thinking how nicely it was burning!

Japanese: ５分ぐらい、見てたかなあ。 そこで‥‥
English: Then after about five minutes, I saw it.

Japanese: 被告人が“飛ぶ”ところを 見た、と‥‥？
English: The defendant "flying"...?

Japanese: そうなんだよ！
English: That's right!

Japanese: だからオレ、橋のところまで 行くことにしたんだ！
English: That's when I decided to go to the bridge!

Japanese: ‥‥なるほど。スジは とおっていますね。
English: ...I see. That all makes sense.

Japanese: この絵、以外は‥‥
English: Aside from the sketch itself...

Japanese: （‥‥そう‥‥この絵は スジがとおらない‥‥）
English: (Yes... This sketch doesn't make any sense...)

Japanese: （なんとしても、 思い知らせてやるのだ‥‥）
English: (I just need to convince the artist that his work is ludicrous.)

Japanese: ‥‥そんなにコーフンしていた 理由は、もしかして‥‥
English: ...Could the reason you were so excited be...?

Japanese: 決まってるだろ！
English: What do you think!?

Japanese: オレのコイビトが、 空ァ飛んじゃったんだぜ！
English: My girlfriend was flying through the sky!

Japanese: それも、橋のはるか上空、 １０メートルぐらいの高さを！
English: Almost 30 feet above the bridge!

Japanese: なんだか、オレも 飛べそうな気がしてさァ。
English: I felt like I might start flying myself.

Japanese: （こいつなら、ホントに イキオイで飛びかねないな‥‥）
English: (If only you'd be so kind as to fly out of all of our lives...!)

Japanese: とにかく、オレ！　ワレを忘れて、 こいつを描いたんだよ！
English: Anyway! I completely lost myself when I was drawing this scene!

Japanese: “見たまま”だと言うのか？ この絵が。
English: You transferred everything "directly onto the page"...? And that is this sketch here?

Japanese: もお、センセーションだろ？ たまらずに、スキャンダルだろ？
English: Yep! Sensational, isn't it? Or should that be scandalous?

Japanese: ‥‥まあ、オレも ナットクしたワケよ。
English: ...It put my mind at ease, anyway.

Japanese: “ナットク”‥‥？
English: It did?

Japanese: そうさ！　オレが待っていた 山小屋に来られなかったのは‥‥
English: That's right! The reason she didn't come to me, even though I waited for her...

Japanese: 橋の向こう側で、足止めされて いたからなんだなあ、って！
English: ...was because she was trapped on the other side of the bridge!

Japanese: もしかして、あやめちゃん‥‥
English: Could it be that Iris...

Japanese: オレに早く会いたいために、 あんなキケンなフライトを‥‥
English: ...undertook that dangerous, desperate flight in order to reach me...!?

Japanese: そうにちがいねえよ！ ヤッホー、オレ！
English: That's gotta be it! Alright! Go, me!

Japanese: やっほぉぉぉ！
English: Nice ooooooouch!

Japanese: ‥‥先をつづけて。
English: ...Continue. Please.

Japanese: 矢張。キサマが事件当夜、 何を見たのか‥‥？
English: Larry. What did you really see that night...?

Japanese: それは、私の 知ったことではない。
English: Not that I particularly care.

Japanese: そんなムセキニンな 言いかたってあるかよ、御剣！
English: In your position, that's just being irresponsible!

Japanese: オレ‥‥ホントに、 見たまんまを描いたんだよ！
English: I... I drew exactly what I saw!

Japanese: １００円までなら かけたっていいぜ！
English: I'll give you a whole dollar that it's the truth!

Japanese: それならば‥‥
English: If that is truly the case...

Japanese: ただ１つだけ、ハッキリ 言えることがあるようだ。
English: Then there is one thing that we can say for certain.

Japanese: それは‥‥なんですかな？
English: What might that be...?

Japanese: 橋を飛びこえた、この人物は‥‥
English: That the person who flew over the bridge...

Japanese: 被告人・あやめさんでは あり得ないッ！
English: ...could not have been the defendant, Iris!

Japanese: な。な。な。なんでだよ！ なんだよ！　わかんねえよ！
English: W-W-What!? What do you mean? I don't understand!?

Japanese: わかかあァッ！
English: Uwaaagh!

Japanese: ‥‥バカがバカなりにバカなコトを 言っているようね‥‥。
English: A foolhardy folly of a foolish statement by an equally foolishly foolhardy fool.

Japanese: いったい、なぜ‥‥ そんなことが断言できるの！
English: How, exactly, can you make this claim?

Japanese: ‥‥いいか、矢張。 この絵によると‥‥
English: ...Tell us, Larry. According to this picture...

Japanese: キサマが見た“人物”は‥‥ ずきんをつけていたようだ。
English: ...the individual whom you say you saw was wearing a hood. Correct?

Japanese: アタリマエだろ！
English: Of course she was!

Japanese: オレのいたボロ小屋から橋までは、 かなりキョリがあったからさあ。
English: That run-down shack is quite a way from the bridge...

Japanese: そのずきんを見たからオレ、 あやめちゃんってわかったんだぜ！
English: The hood is what told me that this floating angel was my Iris!

Japanese: ずきんこそ、我が、あやめちゃん！ あやめちゃんこそ、我が、ずきん！
English: The hood is my darling Iris! And Iris is my darling hood!

Japanese: わかかあァッ！
English: Waaah!

Japanese: 弁護席のバカよりバカな バカがいるようね‥‥
English: It seems there are bigger fools in this world than the one at the defense's bench.

Japanese: ‥‥矢張。これだけは 知っておいたほうがいい。
English: ...Larry, there's something you need to be made aware of.

Japanese: 事件当夜‥‥。あやめさんは ずきんをかぶっていなかった。
English: On the night of the murder... Iris wasn't wearing her hood.

Japanese: 彼女は、それを成歩堂　龍一に プレゼントしていたのだ！
English: She had given it to Wright as a gift!

Japanese: キサマが描いた、ずきんの人物！ 成歩堂だと主張するつもりかッ！
English: Are you going to change your story now? Perhaps suggest it was Wright who took flight!?

Japanese: な‥‥なんだよ、それ‥‥
English: W-What are you talking about!?

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、 リカイしたようだな‥‥。
English: ...I think you understand what I mean just fine.

Japanese: ‥‥なんで‥‥
English: Why...?

Japanese: なんで、あやめちゃんのずきんを 成歩堂が持ってるんだよ‥‥
English: Why did Nick have Iris's hood!?

Japanese: 御剣ィィッ！ ‥‥あの子、アイツのなんなのさ！
English: Edgey! What's going on with Iris and Nick!?

Japanese: ズルいぞおおおお成歩堂おおおお！
English: Why you, Niiiiiick! You dooooooog!

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら‥‥
English: I do believe...

Japanese: この、信じられないような フシギなスケッチは‥‥
English: ...that this unbelievably mysterious sketch...

Japanese: 信じられることのないまま、 ゴミ箱へ消えゆくようですな。
English: ...is destined to disappear, discredited and discarded. Straight into the garbage.

Japanese: クックックックックックックックッ クックックックッ
English: Hah ha ha ha ha ha ha ha! Hah ha ha ha ha ha ha ha!

Japanese: ぎぎぎゃあ！
English: Hagaaah!

Japanese: ‥‥ナニかしら？　証人。
English: ...What is it now, witness?

Japanese: オレ‥‥２５年間。この日を 待っていたような気がするんだ。
English: It feels like... I've been waiting 25 years for this very day to come!

Japanese: 御剣ィィ！　‥‥オマエに ギャフンと言わせる、この日をッ！
English: Edgey! Today is the day I get to completely stupefy you!

Japanese: な‥‥なんだと‥‥？
English: W-What...!?

Japanese: 証人！　どういうコトですか！
English: What is the meaning of your outburst, witness!?

Japanese: ‥‥悪いけどなァ。オレには、 決定的なショーコがあるんだよ。
English: ...I hate to have to do this, but I have some definitive evidence.

Japanese: “決定的な”‥‥？
English: "Definitive"...?

Japanese: “ショーコ”‥‥？
English: "Evidence"...?

Japanese: あのとき‥‥あやめちゃんは、 燃える橋を飛びこえたんだよ！
English: Iris did indeed come flying over the burning bridge!

Japanese: そいつを‥‥このオレが！ 天流斎　マシスな、このオレが！
English: And I, Laurice Deauxnim...

Japanese: ‥‥立証してやるぜええッ！
English: ...shall prove it!

Japanese: ‥‥二度と聞くことも あるまいと思っていましたが‥‥
English: I didn't expect to ask this again...

Japanese: もう一度だけ‥‥ 証言をおねがいしましょう！
English: But we shall be needing your testimony once again.

Japanese: この、いまわしいスケッチに 描かれた、被告人‥‥
English: Tell us anything you know concerning the defendant as depicted in this sketch...

Japanese: これが、悪夢のような現実だったと いう、その証拠について！
English: And show us your evidence that this nightmare was actually a reality!

Japanese: ‥‥いいか。忘れるなよ、御剣ィ。
English: ...OK. I hope you're ready, Edgey!

Japanese: ちゃーんと“ギャフン” て言えよなッ！
English: Because I'm going to feed you a whopping serving of pain!

Japanese: （‥‥この時点で、すでに “ギャフン”なのだが‥‥）
English: (...You've been serving us a whopping serving of pain this whole time. Trust me.)

Japanese: ～あやめが飛んだ証拠！～
English: -- Proof That Iris Flew! --

Japanese: おぼろ橋のたもとに行ったとき、 彼女はもう、いなかったけど‥‥
English: When I reached Dusky Bridge she was already gone...

Japanese: オレ‥‥彼女がシンパイで、 そのへんを探しまわったんだ！
English: I was so worried! So I frantically searched all over for her!

Japanese: そして、雪に半分埋もれかけた、 キレイな水晶を見つけたんだよ！
English: That led to me finding a beautiful crystal sphere, half-buried in the snow!

Japanese: きっと、あやめちゃん‥‥ かわりのずきんをかぶってたのさ。
English: I'm sure that Iris was simply wearing a spare hood.

Japanese: あの晩、水晶をなくしたヤツ、 他にいるワケないだろ？　な。
English: After all, no one else could have lost a crystal sphere that night.

Japanese: す、水晶‥‥ですか！
English: A c-crystal sphere...!?

Japanese: ホラ、コイツさ。 キレイだろ？　‥‥オレのだぜ！
English: This one. Pretty, isn't it? But finders keepers!

Japanese: その水晶‥‥ どこで見つけたのかしら？
English: That sphere... Where did you find it?

Japanese: ま。このへんかな。 ‥‥あえて言うならば。
English: Let me see... Around here, somewhere? Looks about right.

Japanese: “雪に埋もれかけていた”‥‥ というのは？
English: And it was half-buried in the snow...?

Japanese: もう、ほとんど やんでいたけど‥‥
English: It had pretty much stopped snowing by then...

Japanese: おぼろ橋に行くまで、まだ 少し降ってたからなぁ、雪。
English: But there was still some falling as I walked to the bridge.

Japanese: ぬうううううう‥‥ その水晶を、受理します！
English: Hmmmm... The court accepts this crystal sphere!

Japanese: ‥‥オレのだからな！ あとで返してくれよ！
English: ...That's mine, OK!? I want it back afterward!

Japanese: この水晶‥‥ 少し、くもっていますね。
English: There's something on it, isn't there...

Japanese: どうやら‥‥血痕が 付着しているようです！
English: Oh my... It's a blood stain!
(What!? A blood stain...!?)

Japanese: 証拠品《水晶》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Crystal Sphere added to the Court Record.

Japanese: いいな、御剣ィ！
English: You ready, Edgey!?

Japanese: 明日から、オレのコト 《政志さま》って呼べよな！
English: By tomorrow morning, you'll be calling me "Master Larry"!

Japanese: 右から読んでも《マサシサマ》 左から読んでも《マサシサマ》だ！
English: Yeah, I like the sound of that! No one's going to push me around anymore!

Japanese: （‥‥《天流斎　マシス》じゃ なかったのか、キサマは‥‥）
English: (...Didn't you want to be called "Laurice Deauxnim" only a few minutes ago!?)

Japanese: ～あやめが飛んだ証拠！～
English: -- Proof That Iris Flew! --

Japanese: おぼろ橋のたもとに行ったとき、 彼女はもう、いなかったけど‥‥
English: When I reached Dusky Bridge she was already gone...

Japanese: オレ‥‥彼女がシンパイで、 そのへんを探しまわったんだ！
English: I was so worried! So I frantically searched all over for her!

Japanese: そして、雪に半分埋もれかけた、 キレイな水晶を見つけたんだよ！
English: That led to me finding a beautiful crystal sphere, half-buried in the snow!

Japanese: きっと、あやめちゃん‥‥ かわりのずきんをかぶってたのさ。
English: I'm sure that Iris was simply wearing a spare hood.

Japanese: あの晩、水晶をなくしたヤツ、 他にいるワケないだろ？　な。
English: After all, no one else could have lost a crystal sphere that night.

Japanese: （やはり、この事件‥‥ カンタンに、ほどけそうもない）
English: (This case isn't going to end without a fight...)

Japanese: （この証言のムジュンを暴くのは たやすいことだが‥‥）
English: (Exposing the obvious contradictions in this testimony will be easy...)

Japanese: （その先に待つのは、 おそらく‥‥新たなナゾ、だ）
English: (But I fear that all that awaits us are further mysteries!)

Japanese: ‥‥キサマが、燃える橋まで 行ったのは、もしかして‥‥？
English: ...So, you went to the burning bridge?

Japanese: 決まってるだろ！　むかえに 行ったんだよ。あやめちゃんを！
English: That's right! To meet Iris!

Japanese: オレに会いたいからって、 あいつ‥‥ムリしやがって‥‥
English: She actually flew to get to me! The least I could do was meet her halfway!

Japanese: でも‥‥被告人は、橋のたもとには いなかったのですよね？
English: But... the defendant was not at the bridge when you got there, you say?

Japanese: いったん、葉桜院に帰ったんじゃ ねえかなあ。
English: I guess she went back to Hazakura Temple.

Japanese: 女の子だからさぁ。 おめかししに行ったと思うんだよ。
English: She's a girl, after all. She must have wanted to look her best.

Japanese: （‥‥はげしくムジュンしている 気もするが‥‥）
English: (It must be lovely to live in the fantasyland of Larry's mind...)

Japanese: （人差し指を持ち上げる 気力もわいてこないな‥‥）
English: (Actually, it's so depressing that I can't even work up the energy to point...)

Japanese: それで‥‥、そのあと あなたは、どうしたのですか？
English: So... what did you do next?

Japanese: “そのへん”‥‥？
English: So you searched all over for her?

Japanese: なんといっても彼女、かなり 高く飛んだワケだから。
English: She was flying pretty high, you know.

Japanese: 着地のときにスベって、ケガでも したかもしれねえな、って思って。
English: I thought maybe she slipped on her landing and got hurt. Hey! It was more than possible!

Japanese: オレ、あのボロ小屋に行く途中 デザートのバナナ、食っててさあ。
English: Also, when I headed out to the shack the first time, I was snacking on a banana.

Japanese: たしか、その皮をあのへんに 捨てたような気がしたし。
English: I was pretty sure I threw the peel away somewhere around there, so, you know...

Japanese: （バカか、このオトコは‥‥）
English: (Can one guy really be this stupid?)

Japanese: それで‥‥《着地》の跡は 見つかったのか？
English: So, did you find any signs of her so-called "landing"?

Japanese: ううん‥‥ よく、おぼえてねえなァ。
English: Hmm... I don't really remember.

Japanese: それに、オレ自身も何回か転んで、 ワケ、わからなくなっちまって。
English: I kept on falling over myself, and kinda lost it for a while there.

Japanese: 証人が‥‥転んだ？
English: You... fell over yourself?

Japanese: ホレ。雪に足をとられたり、 バナナの皮ですべったりしてさァ！
English: Yeah. The snow was deep, and there was even a banana peel out there!

Japanese: （バカだ、このオトコは‥‥）
English: (Yup. There's stupid, and then there's Larry Butz.)

Japanese: まあ、とにかく。《着地》のアトは 見つからなかった、と。
English: The short of it is that you didn't find any signs of her "landing", correct?

Japanese: それで、どうなったのですか？
English: Then what happened next?

Japanese: “埋もれかけていた”‥‥？
English: "Half-buried"...?

Japanese: 水晶は、雪の中にメリ込んでいて、 少し雪がつもっていたんだよ。
English: It was sitting in the snow, with a little gathered on top of it.

Japanese: ほとんど見えないぐらいだったな。 ‥‥暗かったし。
English: It was very hard to spot, actually. I mean, it was dark out, too.

Japanese: しかし‥‥そんなモノを、 よく見つけましたなあ。
English: I'm impressed... You did well to find it!

Japanese: こう見えてもオレ、プロだから。 天才ガードマンなワケよ。
English: No matter what you may think when you look at me, I'm a pro. A genius security guard.

Japanese: 会社から勝手に借りてきた ペンライトでサッと照らしたら‥‥
English: I used a penlight I "borrowed" from my company to help in my search...

Japanese: 雪の中から“キランッ”て。 『わたしはここよ』‥‥みたいな。
English: And it sparkled really brightly, as if it was saying, "Here I am!" to me.

Japanese: たしかに、あやめさんの ずきんの水晶に似ているが‥‥
English: It does indeed look very much like the crystal sphere on Iris's hood...

Japanese: あの晩‥‥彼女は、ずきんを かぶっていなかったのだぞ。
English: But need I remind you that she was not wearing her hood that night?

Japanese: しかし！　ずきんは、ひとりの僧に ひとつずつしか与えられないのだ！
English: Each nun is assigned their own hood! And they are assigned only one!

Japanese: そんなコト、オレが知るかよ！ あやめちゃんはトクベツなんだ！
English: I don't know anything about that, OK! And Iris is special, alright dude!?

Japanese: かわりのずきん、ちょろまかしても ‥‥オレは、ユルすぜ。
English: Even if she did steal a spare hood... I'll forgive her!

Japanese: （ハナシにならないな‥‥ いつもどおり）
English: (This is getting us nowhere... Our destination for the day, it seems.)

Japanese: しかし‥‥こうして、水晶が 落ちていたのはジジツです。
English: However... this crystal sphere was found near the bridge. That is a fact.

Japanese: ずきんから落ちたのではないと すれば‥‥いったい、どこから？
English: If it didn't come from a hood, where could it have come from?

Japanese: （問題は‥‥それだな）
English: (That is the question I aim to answer.)

Japanese: 最後に、もう一度 カクニンしておこう‥‥矢張。
English: Let me confirm this one last time, Larry.

Japanese: キサマが、この人物を 《あやめさん》と思った理由は‥‥
English: The reasons you thought that this was "Iris"...

Japanese: 《ずきん》と、この 《水晶》だったわけだな？
English: ...are the hood and this crystal sphere, correct?

Japanese: そうさ！　オレのカンに マチガイはないぜ！
English: That's right! My gut is never wrong!

Japanese: 次の日の朝、ビキニのオバサンに 会ったけど‥‥
English: I met that old Bikini the next morning...

Japanese: ちゃーんと、ブラ下がってたぜ。 彼女のずきんに、水晶。
English: And her crystal sphere was still there, safe and sound!

Japanese: ‥‥たしかに‥‥。
English: Indeed...

Japanese: さっきの尋問でも つけていましたな。
English: She was wearing it in this very room, earlier today.

Japanese: ‥‥矢張。
English: ...Larry.

Japanese: あの晩‥‥ ひとりだけ、いたのだ。
English: That night... There was someone.

Japanese: 水晶を失った人物‥‥が。
English: Someone who lost a crystal sphere.
Th-There was...?

Japanese: ダレだよ！ そんなマヌケなバカは！
English: Who!? Who was this stupid idiot!?

Japanese: 天流斎　エリス！ ‥‥マヌケなバカのキミの師匠だ。
English: Ms. Elise Deauxnim! The mentor to a stupid idiot!

Japanese: 被害者‥‥ですって‥‥？
English: The victim...!?

Japanese: ここに、彼女の写真がある。
English: I have a photo of her here.

Japanese: ‥‥ツエの先に、 よく似た水晶がついている‥‥
English: And on the end of her staff, you can see a familiar looking crystal sphere.

Japanese: あッ、それ‥‥
English: H-Hey...!

Japanese: その写真、オレのじゃねえか！ ‥‥返せッ！
English: That's my photograph! ...Give it back!

Japanese: ひひぃぃィッ！
English: Ou-Ou-Ouuuuch!

Japanese: ‥‥ありふれた水晶など、 どこにでも転がっているわ。
English: ...A crystal sphere like that is quite easy to find.

Japanese: この私だって、エリもとに そっくりの水晶をつけている。
English: I have one just like it on my broach.

Japanese: （ゼンゼン似てないッ！）
English: (They look nothing alike!)

Japanese: とにかく‥‥こちらを 見ていただこう。
English: In any case... please take a look at this.

Japanese: 現場から発見された、 被害者のツエだ！
English: This is the victim's staff, found at the scene of the crime!

Japanese: ‥‥ああああッ！ 水晶が‥‥消えていますッ！
English: ...Aaaaah! The crystal sphere... It's gone!
W-W-W-W...

Japanese: なんだよ！　なんなんだよ！ なんでだよ！　なんだったんだよ！
English: What!? What!? What!? What!?

Japanese: わかんねえよおおおおおおおおッ！
English: WHAAAAAAAAAAT!?

Japanese: い‥‥いったい‥‥これは！ どういうことなのですかッ！
English: J-Just what does this mean!?

Japanese: もし、あの晩‥‥ 橋を飛び越えた者がいたとして‥‥
English: If anyone jumped... or flew across the bridge that night...

Japanese: それは、あやめさんではなかった！ なぜならば‥‥
English: ...it certainly was not Iris! After all...

Japanese: 彼女は、ずきんをかぶって いなかった！　そして‥‥
English: She was not wearing her hood! More importantly...

Japanese: 《着地地点》で発見された水晶も、 彼女のものではなかったからだ！
English: ...the crystal sphere found at the "landing site" was not hers either!

Japanese: そ‥‥それでは！ 空を飛んだのは‥‥
English: T-That means... The one who flew...

Japanese: 水晶を落とした被害者‥‥ 天流斎　エリスさんですかッ！
English: ...and dropped the sphere... was the victim, Ms. Elise Deauxnim!?

Japanese: バカとバカのバカなギロンは、 必然的にバカげた結論に達する‥‥
English: A fool alongside another fool, on a fool's errand to reach a foolish conclusion!

Japanese: ‥‥まず、その《スケッチ》と 称する、ラクガキ‥‥
English: First of all, this "sketch", which I prefer to call a scribble, is ridiculous!

Japanese: ヒトは、空を飛べないわ。 ‥‥したがって、却下。
English: People cannot fly! ...Thus, it is rejected!

Japanese: そ‥‥そんなのアリかよ！ だってオレ、見たんだし
English: Y-You can't do that! I saw it! With my own two--

Japanese: たはははぁッ！
English: Eyeeeeeeiiiz!

Japanese: ‥‥そして、この水晶‥‥
English: ...And this crystal sphere.

Japanese: こんなもの、目くらましに すぎないわ。
English: This is nothing more than a red herring!

Japanese: “目くらまし”‥‥だと？
English: You honestly believe that...?

Japanese: 少し考えれば、わかることよ。 ‥‥御剣　怜侍。
English: Give it some thought, and I'm sure you'll realize it as well, Miles Edgeworth!

Japanese: 天流斎　エリスは‥‥事件当夜、 葉桜院の自室にいたのよ。
English: Elise Deauxnim was in her room on the night of the murder.

Japanese: 当然、おぼろ橋のたもとへ 行く理由など、存在しない。
English: There was no reason for her to go to Dusky Bridge!

Japanese: したがって‥‥この水晶も、 事件とは無関係。‥‥以上よ。
English: Therefore, this sphere cannot be related to this case. That is all.

Japanese: ‥‥狩魔　冥。
English: ...Ms. Franziska von Karma.

Japanese: それでナットクするのは、 よほどのお調子者だけだ。
English: The only people who will accept that explanation... are scatterbrains and clowns!

Japanese: なんでそこで オレを指さすんだよ！
English: Why are you pointing at me!?

Japanese: 被害者の水晶が‥‥事件当夜、 橋のたもとから発見されたのだ。
English: The victim's crystal sphere was found, near the bridge, on the night of her murder.

Japanese: それが、事件と無関係だとは 考えられないッ！
English: Yet you expect us to believe this has nothing to do with the case!?

Japanese: その水晶なら、おそらく‥‥
English: That crystal sphere...

Japanese: 事件の起こった後‥‥ 橋のたもとに捨てられたのよ。
English: It was probably thrown away at the bridge after the murder.

Japanese: “事件が起こった後”‥‥ですか？
English: After the murder?

Japanese: 水晶には、わずかに 血痕が残っている。
English: There is blood on the crystal sphere, isn't there?

Japanese: 殺害の《後》、捨てられたと 考えるほうが自然でしょう？
English: This naturally suggests that it was thrown away after the murder took place.

Japanese: 犯人は、捜査の目を くらますために、水晶を捨てて‥‥
English: The killer placed it there to throw the investigation off the scent...

Japanese: 同じ理由で、くだらない スケッチを用意したのよ！
English: The same reason that he drew that ridiculous sketch!

Japanese: ん？
English: What...?

Japanese: それって‥‥オレがハンニン、 ってコトじゃねえか！
English: You mean... I'm the killer!?

Japanese: めひぃぃィッ！
English: No waaaagh!

Japanese: 冗談はともかく‥‥
English: Enough joking...

Japanese: この水晶が、いつ‥‥ 橋のたもとに現れたか？
English: Just when did this crystal sphere appear near the foot of the bridge?

Japanese: それが立証できなければ、 事件との関連は認められないわ。
English: Unless this can be proven in some way, I refuse to believe this is related to the case!

Japanese: （たしかに、あの晩‥‥）
English: (She makes a valid point...)

Japanese: （天流斎　エリスが、葉桜院を はなれたという証拠はない）
English: (There is no evidence that Elise Deauxnim left Hazakura Temple that night.)

Japanese: （‥‥しかし。 もし、この水晶が‥‥）
English: (...However, if somehow this crystal sphere...)

Japanese: （被害者が殺害される《前》に 落とされたのだとしたら‥‥）
English: (...can be proven to have been dropped before the victim was killed...)

Japanese: （‥‥この事件は、まったくちがう 姿を見せることになる‥‥！）
English: (...then this case is going to transform into something else entirely!)

Japanese: いかがですか、弁護人。
English: Your response, Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: 水晶について‥‥ 最終的な意見をうかがいましょう！
English: I want your final opinion on the disposition of this crystal sphere!

Japanese: もし、事件に無関係ならば！ あなたの呼んだ、この証人‥‥
English: If it is not related to the case, then this witness who you called...

Japanese: 壮大な時間のムダだった、 ということになります！
English: ...will have been nothing more than a monumental waste of time!

Japanese: 相当のペナルティを カクゴすることね‥‥御剣　怜侍。
English: Prepare yourself for some very appropriate punishment, Miles Edgeworth.

Japanese: （この私に、立証できるか‥‥？）
English: (Can I prove it...?)

Japanese: （水晶が‥‥事件の起こる 《前》に落とされた、と‥‥）
English: (Can I prove that the crystal sphere was dropped before the murder took place...?)

Japanese: （‥‥やはり、 どう考えてもムリだ‥‥）
English: (No matter how I look at it, it's not possible...)

Japanese: （たしかに、水晶が橋のたもとに 落ちているのは、不自然）
English: (It is certainly strange that the crystal sphere was found near the bridge.)

Japanese: （犯人の細工、と考える べきなのかもしれない‥‥）
English: (But perhaps it is just a curve ball from the killer.)

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら‥‥ 弁護側にも、異議はないようね。
English: Well, well... Miles Edgeworth seems to have nothing further to say.

Japanese: ぬううううううううう‥‥ いたしかたありませんな。
English: Hmmmmmm... Then I am left with no choice.

Japanese: それでは、以上で‥‥
English: The court sees no reason...

Japanese: なんだよそれ！
English: What is this!?

Japanese: オイ、御剣！　オマエ、 弁護士だろうがよ！
English: Hey, Edgey! You're a defense attorney, aren't you!?

Japanese: ‥‥こんなとき、成歩堂なら‥‥
English: If it was Nick standing where you are right now...

Japanese: こんなとき、成歩堂なら‥‥ ゼッタイ、あきらめねえぜ！
English: If it was Nick, do you think he'd give up!?

Japanese: ムリヤリでも、ホントのコトを 見つけてくれるハズさ！
English: No! He'd find the truth, no matter what it took! No matter how crazy it seemed!

Japanese: （そうだったな、成歩堂‥‥）
English: (Wright...)

Japanese: （私は今‥‥、“キミ”として この場にいるのだった）
English: (At this moment, I'm standing where you should be.)

Japanese: （‥‥それならば！　私のするべき ことは、１つしかない！）
English: (Which means there's only one thing to do! Think like you!)

Japanese: （“立証できるか”‥‥？ そんなことは、問題ではない）
English: ("Can I prove it...?" That isn't the issue.)

Japanese: （“立証する” ‥‥それしかないではないか）
English: (To simply "prove it". That's the only option!)

Japanese: （それが、あのオトコ‥‥）
English: (That's what he'd do...)

Japanese: （‥‥成歩堂　龍一の やり方なのだから！）
English: (That's the way Phoenix Wright would do this!)

Japanese: ‥‥裁判長。 立証してごらんに入れよう。
English: ...Your Honor. Allow me to prove something to you.

Japanese: 水晶が、この殺人事件にとって、 大きなイミを持っていることを！
English: I will prove that this crystal sphere is a vital link to solving this case!

Japanese: な‥‥なんですと‥‥！
English: You will do what!?

Japanese: ‥‥あなたの、その目‥‥
English: That look in your eyes...

Japanese: 追いつめられた、 成歩堂　龍一を思い出すわ。
English: You remind me of Phoenix Wright when he is cornered.

Japanese: ‥‥当然だろうな‥‥
English: That should come as no surprise.

Japanese: （私は今、追いつめられた 成歩堂　龍一なのだから‥‥！）
English: (Because right now, I am Phoenix Wright, and I am indeed cornered...!)

Japanese: それでは、弁護人に 証拠品の提示を命じます！
English: I order you to present your evidence, Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: 水晶が、この事件の起こる《前》に 落とされたことを立証する証拠を！
English: Evidence that proves that the crystal sphere was indeed dropped before the murder!

Japanese: ‥‥いかがですかな？ 狩魔検事。
English: ...Your response, Ms. von Karma?

Japanese: 《類は友を呼ぶ》‥‥ この国では、そう言うそうね。
English: Birds of a feather flock together... Are you familiar with this phrase?

Japanese: ‥‥御剣　怜侍。
English: Miles Edgeworth!

Japanese: お友だちが、あなたを 呼んでいるみたいよ。
English: I think one such bird is calling for its fellow now!

Japanese: よおォ、御剣ィ！ しっかりやれよォ。
English: Go, go, Edgey! Do it! Prove it! Win! Win! Win!

Japanese: （コイツにはげまされるとは‥‥ もう、オシマイだッ！）
English: (His cheering... It's as though I'm listening to the ominous caws of ravens!)

Japanese: ‥‥さて、弁護人。 どうしますかな？
English: ...So, Mr. Edgeworth. What will you do from here?

Japanese: も‥‥もう一度だ！ このままでは帰れぬッ！
English: O-One more chance, Your Honor. I can't turn back after coming all this way.

Japanese: （被害者が殺害された時刻と、 水晶が落とされた時刻‥‥）
English: (The time of the murder and when the crystal sphere was dropped...)

Japanese: （どちらが早かったか、立証できる 証拠を探すしかない‥‥！）
English: (I need to find proof that the latter happened first!)

Japanese: ‥‥この水晶‥‥ 《半分、雪に埋もれていた》とか。
English: This crystal sphere... It was half-buried in the snow, correct?

Japanese: ああ。もう少し雪がやむのが 遅かったら、アブなかったぜ。
English: That's right. If it hadn't stopped snowing then it would have been game over.

Japanese: ‥‥コイツ、完全に雪で かくれちまっただろうからな！
English: ...The snow would have totally covered it!

Japanese: それで、じゅうぶんだ‥‥矢張。
English: ...That's all I needed to hear from you, Larry.

Japanese: ‥‥今の証言から、 アキラカなことが１つ、ある。
English: ...Your testimony makes one thing quite clear.

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥？
English: W-What...?

Japanese: 水晶が落とされたとき、 少しだが、雪は降っていた‥‥。
English: When the crystal sphere was dropped, it was snowing, even if it was ever so slightly...
Snowing...?

Japanese: そして、一方‥‥ 殺人現場を見てみよう。
English: On the other hand, let us look at the scene of the murder.

Japanese: 先ほども立証したとおり。 被害者には、雪がつもっていない。
English: As proven earlier today... there is no snow on the victim's body.

Japanese: ‥‥つまり！
English: ...Therefore!

Japanese: 水晶は、殺人事件よりも《前》に 落とされたことになるのだッ！
English: The crystal sphere must have been dropped before the murder!
Wha...

Japanese: なんですってェェェッ！
English: Whaaaaat!?

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛にッ！
English: Order! Order! Order!

Japanese: 被害者は、事件当夜！ おぼろ橋まで、行ったのだ！
English: On the night of the murder the victim did indeed go to Dusky Bridge!

Japanese: そして、そこで‥‥ツエの水晶が はずれるようなコトが起こった！
English: And there, something occurred that caused this crystal sphere to come loose!

Japanese: い、いったい‥‥何が！
English: What... What could that have been!?

Japanese: この水晶‥‥血痕で少し、 くもっているようだな‥‥
English: This sphere... There is some blood on it, isn't there?

Japanese: ここで‥‥ある、ひとつの 可能性が浮かび上がる。
English: Allow me to raise a certain possibility at this junction!

Japanese: 《本当の犯行現場は、 おぼろ橋のたもとだった》！
English: The real crime scene was near the foot of Dusky Bridge!

Japanese: ‥‥殺人現場が 葉桜院の境内ではなかった‥‥？
English: The murder didn't take place in the Hazakura Temple courtyard...!?

Japanese: バカがバカにふさわしく バカなコトを言っているようね‥‥
English: Only a fool would suggest such a foolish piece of absolute foolishness!

Japanese: ‥‥だれがバカで、どのあたりが バカなコト、なのだろうか‥‥？
English: Just who is the fool? And which part is so foolish, Ms. von Karma?

Japanese: いいかしら？　御剣　怜侍！
English: Have you been paying any attention this whole time, Miles Edgeworth!?

Japanese: ジューショクさまは、 ハッキリ、目撃しているのよ！
English: The Sister saw everything!

Japanese: 葉桜院の境内で、被告人が 被害者を殺害しているところを！
English: She saw the victim being killed by the defendant in the Hazakura Temple courtyard!

Japanese: ‥‥それは、ジジツとは ちがうようだ。
English: ...That's not exactly true, now is it?

Japanese: 彼女が目撃した、光景。 それは、正確には‥‥
English: To put it more precisely, what she saw was...

Japanese: 《被害者の身体から、 凶器を抜いたところ》なのだ。
English: ...the murder weapon being removed from the victim's body.

Japanese: お‥‥同じコトでしょ！
English: Th-That's the same thing!

Japanese: ‥‥ちがうッ！
English: ...No, it isn't!

Japanese: ‥‥キミは、言ったはずだ。
English: ...You said it yourself.

Japanese: 刃物による刺し傷の場合‥‥
English: Very little blood is actually lost...

Japanese: 刺されたときの出血量‥‥ これは、たいしたことはない。
English: ...at the moment of a blade's insertion.

Japanese: 傷口から、もっとも 出血がはげしいのは‥‥
English: If you want to talk about when the most blood would be lost from a body...

Japanese: ‥‥凶器の刃物が、 傷口から引き抜かれたときよ。
English: ...that would be when the blade is removed.

Japanese: ‥‥そのコトバが本当ならば‥‥
English: If that statement is the truth...

Japanese: 《犯行現場はおぼろ橋だった》 そう考えても、なんの問題もない。
English: ...then Dusky Bridge could very easily be the scene of the murder.

Japanese: 凶器は、引き抜かれなかった。 だから、出血しなかったのだ！
English: The murder weapon was not removed! Thus, there was no bleeding!

Japanese: ひとつ、ダイジなことを 忘れているわ‥‥御剣　怜侍！
English: You are forgetting one vital thing, Miles Edgeworth!

Japanese: 天流斎　エリスの死体は、 葉桜院で発見されているのよ！
English: Elise Deauxnim's body was found in Hazakura Temple!

Japanese: おぼろ橋から、葉桜院まで‥‥ たっぷり１５分もかかる。
English: On foot it takes 15 minutes to travel from Dusky Bridge to Hazakura Temple.

Japanese: そんな長いキョリ、 死体を運べるわけがないッ！
English: You mean to suggest someone carried the body all that way!?

Japanese: （ここまで来たら‥‥ 行けるところまで、行くのみ！）
English: (I've made it this far... The only place to take this is to the end!)

Japanese: （‥‥とにかく、 《可能性》を提示するんだ！）
English: (I just need to prove the possibility it happened as I presume...!)

Japanese: それでは、弁護人。 提示していただきましょう！
English: Now, if the defense is ready, the court would like to have an explanation.

Japanese: 被害者の遺体が、おぼろ橋から 葉桜院に運ばれた、その方法を！
English: Please show us the method by which the victim's body was carried to Hazakura Temple!

Japanese: （ちがう！　そうではない‥‥）
English: (No... That isn't it...)

Japanese: （現場には‥‥その《痕跡》が 残っているではないか！）
English: (I'm on the wrong track here.)

Japanese: （とにかく、今は このまま、突き進むしかない！）
English: (I need to charge on through this...)

Japanese: （‥‥それが、私の依頼人にとって 《不利》に見えようとも‥‥）
English: (Even if it makes things look bad for my client...!)

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人。
English: ...Try again, Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: そんなコワい目で、 私をニラまないように！
English: And this time, without the scary glare, if you don't mind!

Japanese: あの、雪の夜‥‥
English: On that snowy night...

Japanese: 死体を運ぶ方法が、 ただ１つだけ、存在した。
English: ...there is one way that a body could have been moved.

Japanese: ‥‥スノーモービルだ。
English: The snowmobile.

Japanese: 現に‥‥このとおり、事件当夜 スノーモービルは使用されている。
English: As we know... the snowmobile was used that night.

Japanese: 《凶器を捨てに行った》 キミはたしか、そう説明したが‥‥
English: It was explained as having been used to dispose of the murder weapon...

Japanese: 《死体を運んできた》 そうとも考えられるはずだッ！
English: But it could also have been used to carry a body!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！ きゃああああッ！
English: Order! Order! Order! Gyaaaaah!

Japanese: み‥‥認めない‥‥ 認めないわ、御剣　怜侍ッ！
English: This... This is completely unacceptable, Miles Edgeworth!

Japanese: どうやら、 墓穴を掘ったようね！
English: You've dug yourself into your own grave!

Japanese: どういうこと、だ？
English: What do you mean!?

Japanese: スノーモービルを使えたのは、 被告人だけなのよ。
English: The only one who could have used the snowmobile was the defendant.

Japanese: 死体を運んだのは、彼女！ ‥‥それでキマリじゃないかしら？
English: She's the one who moved the body! Doesn't that put the final nail in your coffin...?
Heh...

Japanese: もう、遅いのだ‥‥狩魔　冥。
English: You're too late... Franziska von Karma.

Japanese: 弁護側は、すでに 立証してしまったのだよ。
English: And in fact, the defense has proven something else entirely.

Japanese: ‥‥この事件には、まだ 捜査が必要であることを！
English: We have shown that this case requires further investigation!

Japanese: な‥‥なんですって‥‥？
English: W-What!?

Japanese: ‥‥天流斎　エリスが殺害された、 本当の現場はどこだったか？
English: ...Where was the victim, Elise Deauxnim really killed?

Japanese: ‥‥死体が運ばれたのならば、 その理由はいったい、何か‥‥？
English: ...If her body was moved, whatever for?

Japanese: そして、もうひとつ。
English: And finally...

Japanese: ‥‥この光景は、いったい 何をイミするのか‥‥？
English: Just what does this image mean?

Japanese: そんなこと‥‥ 考えるまでもないッ！
English: Do you even need to think about that!?

Japanese: 愚か者は、真実を見る目も それを語る口も持たないッ！
English: Such a creature could never see the truth, let alone describe it!

Japanese: ‥‥この証人は、たしかに はかり知れない、ろくでなしだ。
English: ...This witness certainly sits on one of the lowest possible branches of humanity.

Japanese: しかし！　好意をよせる女性を キズつけるウソだけは、つかない。
English: However! He would never utter a lie that could hurt a girl with whom he is enamored!

Japanese: 彼が、これを描いた以上‥‥ 現実に起こったこと、なのだ。
English: He drew this, so it is something that actually happened.

Japanese: ‥‥弁護側は、この主張を ゆずるつもりはない。
English: The defense stands firm on this point.

Japanese: み‥‥‥御剣ィィ。オマエ‥‥
English: Ed-Edgey! Thank you...!

Japanese: ‥‥結論が出たようです。
English: ...That settles it, then.

Japanese: 今の時点で、判決を 下すことはできません！
English: I cannot give a verdict under these circumstances.

Japanese: ぐ‥‥‥ぐぐううぅぅぅぅッ！
English: Grrr... Grrrrrr!
(Wright...)

Japanese: （‥‥どうやら、私の役目は 果たせたようだな‥‥）
English: (I seem to have fulfilled my part in this...)

Japanese: やはり、私の思ったとおりだった。
English: It is just as I thought.

Japanese: ‥‥狩魔　冥。 キミは、最高のパートナーだ。
English: Franziska von Karma... You make a wonderful partner.
Excuse me...?

Japanese: 今日、私がここに立った 目的は、ただ１つ。
English: There was one reason, and one alone, for me being here.

Japanese: この事件の闇を引きずり出し、 成歩堂に引きわたすことだった。
English: To expose the darkness lurking in this case, and then pass it on to Wright!

Japanese: な‥‥‥なんだよ！ そういうコトだったのかよ！
English: R-Really? That's what this was all about?

Japanese: それならそうと、 先に言っといてくれよなー！
English: You could have just told me that from the very beginning!

Japanese: そうしたら、オレも 冥ちゃんに、ぶたれずに‥‥
English: Then I wouldn't have had Franzy whipping me all...

Japanese: ぷぎゃああッ！
English: Daaaaaayyyowch!

Japanese: ‥‥御剣　怜侍ィィィ‥‥
English: Miles Edgeworth...

Japanese: あなたのツゴウなど、 知ったことではないわ！
English: I don't care about what you were here to do!

Japanese: 今日こそは‥‥あなたを ひねりつぶすつもりだったのに！
English: This was my chance to finally grind you under my heel!

Japanese: そいつはザンネンだったな。
English: A shame that your chance seems to have slipped by you.

Japanese: ザンネンだったなァ、冥ちゃん。 ぷぎゃああッ！
English: What a shame, Franzzzyyyyyyyy!

Japanese: ‥‥キサマのせいよッ！
English: ...This is all your fault!

Japanese: こんなヒドい証人‥‥ 断じて、あり得ないッ！
English: Such a terrible witness... You are an affront to all the legal system stands for!

Japanese: いてててていてていててててていて ててていててていててていてていて
English: Ouuuouuuouooooouuuuuou ouuooououooouuuuouoouuch!

Japanese: そして、トドメよッ！
English: I demand satisfaction!

Japanese: ‥‥証人の証言は、たしかに 現実のものとは思えません。
English: ...I cannot believe that the witness's testimony relates to an actual event.

Japanese: しかし‥‥そこには、なんらかの 《コタエ》があるはずです。
English: However, there has to be some sort of answer for the questions it raises.

Japanese: 証人のコトバは《真実》なのか、 《いつわり》なのか‥‥？
English: Have his words here today been the truth or lies?

Japanese: ‥‥すべては、次回。
English: Next time we gather in this courtroom...

Japanese: ふたたび、この法廷で うかがうことになるでしょう。
English: ...those are the matters that shall be addressed!

Japanese: 検察側・弁護側の調査に キタイしています。
English: I am counting on thorough investigations by both the defense and the prosecution.
(And with this...)

Japanese: （あとは、キミしだいだ。 成歩堂‥‥）
English: (The rest is up to you... Wright...)

Japanese: それでは！ 本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: Court is now adjourned!

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.

Japanese: そこまで！
English: That's enough!

Japanese: 本法廷は、これ以上の審議の 必要性を認めません。
English: This court sees no reason to further prolong this trial.

Japanese: さらに、被告に対する判決を 決める時間も、必要としません。
English: Nor is there any need for more time to decide the case against the defendant.

Japanese: これは、きわめて明白な事件です。 疑問の余地はない！
English: This case is extremely clear. I see no room for mis- interpretation of the facts.

Japanese: 被告人、葉桜院　あやめに たった今、判決を言いわたします！
English: This court finds the defendant, Iris of Hazakura Temple...

Japanese: 被告人はこれより身柄を 司直に預けるものとし、
English: The accused will surrender to the court immediately...

Japanese: １ヶ月以内に、高等裁判所において 通常裁判を受けることとします。
English: ...to be held pending trial at a higher court within a month from today's date.

Japanese: では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. The court is adjourned!
